S&P Global Mobility.history: S&P Global Mobility was formed in 2022 when S&P Global (the financial data giant, NYSE: SPGI) rebranded the IHS Markit Automotive division following its $44 billion acquisition of IHS Markit. It incorporates the former Dataium automotive shopping analytics platform, providing automotive manufacturers and dealers with market intelligence and consumer behavior data.
S&P Global Mobility.summary_abuse: S&P Global Mobility is an enterprise automotive analytics platform owned by S&P Global with no notable abuse signal. It analyzes automotive consumer shopping data and is used exclusively by automotive industry professionals.

S-COREpion.history: S-COREpion is a Russian PHP and MySQL CMS for building websites and web applications. Very limited public information is available and the project appears to have low ongoing activity.
S-COREpion.summary_abuse: S-COREpion is a small Russian-language self-hosted CMS with minimal public footprint and no documented abuse history.

Saabu.history: Saabu is a CRM automation SaaS for sales teams with limited public history. It is a small, recent product.
Saabu.summary_abuse: Saabu is a CRM platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B sales tool with no documented history of exploitation.

SaaSquatch.history: SaaSquatch is a Canadian referral and loyalty marketing SaaS platform founded around 2012 in Victoria, BC. It offers referral programs, loyalty rewards, and partner marketing tools for growth-stage and enterprise companies.
SaaSquatch.summary_abuse: SaaSquatch is a referral and rewards platform used by legitimate businesses. Referral programs can occasionally be gamed by bad actors seeking to accumulate rewards fraudulently, but this is a general characteristic of referral systems rather than a specific SaaSquatch issue.

Saba.Host.history: Saba.Host is a web hosting provider offering shared hosting, WordPress hosting, VPS, and dedicated servers. It is a small regional hosting provider with limited public history.
Saba.Host.summary_abuse: Saba.Host is a small web hosting provider with modest abuse potential. Web hosting services can be used to host malicious content, but there is no specific documented abuse history for Saba.Host. It appears to be a legitimate regional hosting provider.

SabaVision.history: SabaVision is the advertising platform of SabaIdea, described as Iran's largest online advertising agency. It operates in the Iranian digital advertising market.
SabaVision.summary_abuse: SabaVision is an Iranian online advertising platform. Iranian ad platforms operate in a jurisdiction with different privacy and content standards than Western markets, and US/EU sanctions may restrict legitimate business with them. No specific documented phishing or fraud incidents are associated with SabaVision, but its ad distribution surface carries the standard risks of ad networks including potential malvertisement delivery.

SabeeApp.history: SabeeApp is a Hungarian property management system (PMS) for hotels and accommodation providers, founded around 2013, offering channel management and online booking capabilities.
SabeeApp.summary_abuse: SabeeApp is a hotel property management SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It serves legitimate hospitality businesses and is not known as a platform abused by bad actors.

Saber.history: Saber is an open-source Vue.js-based static site generator, similar to Gatsby or Next.js for static sites. It was created around 2018 and appears to have limited activity in recent years.
Saber.summary_abuse: Saber is a static site generator with no notable abuse signal. It is a developer tool for building static websites and has no consumer-facing surface exploitable by fraudsters.

SafeBase.history: SafeBase is a US-based trust and security review automation SaaS platform founded around 2020. It allows companies to share security documentation and compliance posture publicly or with reviewers, streamlining vendor security reviews.
SafeBase.summary_abuse: SafeBase is a security documentation platform with no notable abuse signal. Its purpose is to improve vendor security transparency, making it an unlikely target or enabler of abuse.

SafeBuy.history: SafeBuy is a UK-based online trust and reviews verification platform that reviews consumer feedback and verifies site links to help shoppers identify legitimate websites.
SafeBuy.summary_abuse: SafeBuy is a trust verification platform for online retailers with no notable abuse signal. Its core purpose is consumer protection, making it an unlikely enabler of scams or fraud.

Saffire.history: Saffire is a US-based SaaS platform offering ticketing and CMS solutions for fairs, festivals, and events. No notable ownership changes documented.
Saffire.summary_abuse: Saffire is a ticketing and events CMS platform serving legitimate event organizers. No notable abuse signal documented.

Sails.js.history: Sails.js is an open-source Node.js MVC web framework created by Mike McNeil in 2012, designed to emulate Ruby on Rails-style conventions for building enterprise-grade Node.js applications. It built on top of Express and was popular in the mid-2010s but has since been overtaken by newer frameworks.
Sails.js.summary_abuse: Sails.js is a backend web framework with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

Sailthru.history: Sailthru was founded in 2008 as a marketing personalization and email automation platform. It was acquired by Campaign Monitor in 2018, and later rebranded under the Marigold brand umbrella, which consolidates multiple email marketing platforms.
Sailthru.summary_abuse: Sailthru is an enterprise email marketing and personalization platform. Like all email marketing platforms, it has potential for spam misuse, but it serves enterprise clients and imposes sender reputation requirements. No specific large-scale abuse incidents are documented.

Sakai.history: Sakai is an open-source learning management system (LMS) created in 2004 by a consortium of universities including Michigan, Indiana, MIT, and Stanford through the Sakai Project. It is now governed by the Apereo Foundation and remains widely used in higher education institutions worldwide.
Sakai.summary_abuse: Sakai is a self-hosted open-source LMS used by universities. It has no notable abuse signal as it is deployed internally by educational institutions with no public consumer-facing surface.

Sakura Internet.history: Sakura Internet is one of Japan's oldest and largest web hosting providers, founded in 1996. It has been publicly listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange and serves a predominantly Japanese market with shared hosting, VPS, and dedicated servers.
Sakura Internet.summary_abuse: Sakura Internet is a Japanese hosting provider. Like all web hosting platforms, it carries inherent risk of being used for spam or phishing by its customers, but is a legitimate and established provider with no specific documented large-scale abuse incidents.

SaleCycle.history: SaleCycle is a UK-based behavioral marketing firm founded around 2010, specializing in cart abandonment recovery and email remarketing for ecommerce.
SaleCycle.summary_abuse: SaleCycle is a behavioral marketing and remarketing platform. It collects user browsing data for targeted remarketing emails, which is ad-tech adjacent and privacy-sensitive. No specific fraud or scam abuse patterns documented.

Saleor.history: Saleor is an open-source headless GraphQL e-commerce platform founded in Poland around 2014. It gained traction as a Python/Django-based commerce solution. The company raised venture capital and offers a cloud-hosted version alongside the open-source code under a Business Source License.
Saleor.summary_abuse: Saleor is a headless ecommerce platform with no notable abuse signal. It is used to build legitimate online stores and its self-hosted nature limits the vendor's exposure to hosted platform abuse.

Sales Auotomator.history: Sales Automator is a sales automation SaaS (listed with a typo as Sales Auotomator in detection databases). It is a small product with limited public information.
Sales Auotomator.summary_abuse: Sales Automator is a sales automation tool with no notable abuse signal. Sales automation platforms can be misused for spam, but there is no specific documented abuse history for this product.

Salesbeat.history: Salesbeat is an ecommerce optimization platform for improving user engagement and conversion rates. It is a small product with limited public history.
Salesbeat.summary_abuse: Salesbeat is an ecommerce enhancement tool with no notable abuse signal. It is a merchant-facing product with no documented history of exploitation.

SalesCandy.history: SalesCandy is a Malaysian sales performance improvement SaaS platform designed to optimize sales team lead distribution and management.
SalesCandy.summary_abuse: SalesCandy is a small sales management SaaS with no documented abuse signal.

SalesFire.history: SalesFire is a UK-based SaaS company specializing in conversion rate optimization and intelligent on-site search for ecommerce retailers.
SalesFire.summary_abuse: SalesFire is a conversion optimization and search SaaS for ecommerce with no notable abuse signal.

Salesfloor.history: Salesfloor is a Canadian mobile clienteling and virtual selling platform designed for retail brands to enable personalized shopping experiences through associates.
Salesfloor.summary_abuse: Salesfloor is a retail clienteling SaaS with no notable abuse signal.

Salesforce.history: Salesforce was founded in 1999 by Marc Benioff, Parker Harris, Dave Moellenhoff, and Frank Dominguez in San Francisco. It pioneered the SaaS model for enterprise CRM and went public in 2004. It has grown through numerous major acquisitions including Tableau (2019), Slack (2021), and MuleSoft (2018), becoming the world's largest CRM platform.
Salesforce.summary_abuse: Salesforce is a dominant enterprise CRM platform with robust security controls and compliance certifications. It is not a preferred vector for abuse and has strong AUP enforcement. Occasional phishing emails may impersonate Salesforce branding, but the platform itself is not a significant abuse enabler.

Salesforce Audience Studio.history: Salesforce Audience Studio was formerly known as Salesforce DMP (Data Management Platform), originally acquired as Krux Digital in 2016. It was a customer data platform for audience segmentation and advertising. Salesforce announced in 2022 that it would wind down Audience Studio, effectively deprecating the product.
Salesforce Audience Studio.summary_abuse: Salesforce Audience Studio was a data management and advertising platform with ad-tech characteristics, collecting user data for targeting. It has been deprecated by Salesforce. No specific fraud abuse patterns documented.

Salesforce Commerce Cloud.history: Salesforce Commerce Cloud originated from Demandware, an enterprise ecommerce platform founded in 2004, which Salesforce acquired in 2016 for $2.8 billion. It was rebranded as Salesforce Commerce Cloud and integrated with the broader Salesforce platform, becoming a leading SaaS solution for B2C and B2B commerce.
Salesforce Commerce Cloud.summary_abuse: Salesforce Commerce Cloud is a managed enterprise ecommerce platform with strong PCI-DSS compliance. It powers legitimate large-scale online retail operations and is not a notable abuse vector.

Salesforce Desk.history: Salesforce Desk, formerly known as Desk.com, was a customer support SaaS acquired by Salesforce in 2011. It was an SMB-focused help desk product. Salesforce officially deprecated Desk.com in 2020, directing customers to migrate to Salesforce Service Cloud.
Salesforce Desk.summary_abuse: Salesforce Desk was a customer support platform with no notable abuse signal. It has been deprecated and replaced by Salesforce Service Cloud.

Salesforce Experience Cloud.history: Salesforce Experience Cloud (formerly Community Cloud) is a Salesforce product launched around 2013 for building branded digital experiences, portals, and communities on top of Salesforce CRM data.
Salesforce Experience Cloud.summary_abuse: Salesforce Experience Cloud is a CRM-powered portal builder used by enterprises. No notable abuse signal documented.

Salesforce Interaction Studio.history: Salesforce Interaction Studio was originally founded as Evergage in 2012, a real-time personalization platform. Salesforce acquired Evergage in 2020 and rebranded it as Interaction Studio, integrating it with the Salesforce Marketing Cloud for real-time website and email personalization.
Salesforce Interaction Studio.summary_abuse: Salesforce Interaction Studio is a personalization and behavioral tracking platform. Like all behavioral analytics products, it collects detailed user activity data. As an enterprise Salesforce product, it has strong compliance controls and is not a known vector for fraud.

Salesforce Marketing Cloud Account Engag.history: Salesforce Marketing Cloud Account Engagement was formerly known as Pardot, a B2B marketing automation platform founded in 2006. Salesforce acquired Pardot in 2013 as part of the ExactTarget acquisition. It was rebranded as Marketing Cloud Account Engagement in 2022.
Salesforce Marketing Cloud Account Engag.summary_abuse: Salesforce Marketing Cloud Account Engagement is a B2B marketing automation and email platform. Like all email marketing platforms, spam misuse is theoretically possible, but it enforces sender policies and serves enterprise clients. No specific large-scale abuse documented.

Salesforce Marketing Cloud Email Studio.history: Salesforce Marketing Cloud Email Studio is part of the Salesforce Marketing Cloud, which originated from ExactTarget, an email marketing company founded in 2000 that was acquired by Salesforce in 2013 for $2.5 billion. It remains the email component of the broader Marketing Cloud suite.
Salesforce Marketing Cloud Email Studio.summary_abuse: Salesforce Marketing Cloud Email Studio is an enterprise email marketing platform. Enterprise email platforms can be misused for spam, but Salesforce enforces sender policies and reputation requirements for its marketing cloud customers.

Salesforce Service Cloud.history: Salesforce Service Cloud is a customer service CRM module that is part of the core Salesforce platform. It evolved from Salesforce's core CRM product and has been significantly expanded through acquisitions and organic development since the late 2000s.
Salesforce Service Cloud.summary_abuse: Salesforce Service Cloud is an enterprise customer service CRM with strong compliance controls and no notable abuse signal.

Salesloft.history: Salesloft was founded in 2011 in Atlanta, Georgia. It is a cloud-based sales engagement platform that went public in 2021 and later merged with Drift in 2023 under the Salesloft brand.
Salesloft.summary_abuse: Salesloft is an enterprise sales engagement platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves legitimate sales organizations and is not a vector for fraud.

Salesmachine.history: Salesmachine is a French customer success and revenue intelligence SaaS. It helps SaaS companies boost sales and reduce churn using data-driven strategies.
Salesmachine.summary_abuse: Salesmachine is a customer success CRM with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B product for SaaS companies with no documented history of exploitation.

SALESmanago.history: SALESmanago was founded in 2011 in Krakow, Poland as a marketing automation and CDP platform targeting mid-sized businesses and enterprises. It is one of the largest marketing automation vendors in Central and Eastern Europe. No major acquisition events are documented.
SALESmanago.summary_abuse: SALESmanago is a B2B marketing automation and customer data platform. As a heavy ad-tech and email marketing tool, it processes substantial customer behavioral data, and like most marketing automation platforms, carries a general risk of being used for spam campaigns. No specific documented abuse incidents are known.

SalesMatch.history: SalesMatch is an AI-powered CRM and business automation platform. It is a small, recent product with limited public history.
SalesMatch.summary_abuse: SalesMatch is a CRM automation platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a small B2B tool with no documented history of exploitation.

Salesnauts.history: Salesnauts is a fashion-focused ecommerce platform. It is a niche vertical ecommerce solution with limited public history.
Salesnauts.summary_abuse: Salesnauts is a fashion ecommerce platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a vertical-specific ecommerce product with no documented history of exploitation.

Salespype.history: Salespype is a CRM and marketing automation platform for sales teams. Very limited public founding history is available.
Salespype.summary_abuse: Salespype is a CRM and sales automation platform with no documented abuse history. It is a B2B tool used by sales teams and does not expose a general consumer-facing hosting surface.

SalesReps.io.history: SalesReps.io is a sales rep performance and commission tracking SaaS. It is a small product focused on sales analytics with limited public history.
SalesReps.io.summary_abuse: SalesReps.io is a sales analytics tool with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B performance management product with no documented history of exploitation.

SalesViewer.history: SalesViewer is a German B2B website visitor identification SaaS tool that reveals which companies visit a website. Founded around 2014.
SalesViewer.summary_abuse: SalesViewer is a B2B visitor identification tool that identifies company visitors to websites. This type of technology is sometimes used without website visitor consent, raising privacy concerns, but its primary market is legitimate sales intelligence for B2B companies.

SalesWings.history: SalesWings is a Belgian lead scoring and sales intelligence SaaS platform that helps identify high-quality sales leads through website behavioral tracking.
SalesWings.summary_abuse: SalesWings is a lead scoring SaaS with no notable abuse signal.

Salla.history: Salla is a Saudi Arabian ecommerce platform founded around 2017, tailored specifically to serve Arabic-speaking merchants in the Middle East and North Africa region.
Salla.summary_abuse: Salla is a regional ecommerce platform for Arabic-speaking markets. Like all ecommerce platforms, it could potentially be used to set up fraudulent storefronts, but it is a legitimate business focused on the MENA market with no specific documented abuse incidents.

Salonist.history: Salonist is an Indian salon and spa management software SaaS founded around 2016, offering scheduling, billing, and inventory management for beauty businesses.
Salonist.summary_abuse: Salonist is a salon management SaaS with no notable abuse signal.

Salsify.history: Salsify was founded in 2012 in Boston by Jason Purcell, Jeremy Redburn, and Rob Gonzalez as a product experience management (PXM) platform. The company raised $453M including a $200M Series F in 2022 at a $2B valuation, with customers including L'Oreal, Walmart, and Coca-Cola.
Salsify.summary_abuse: Salsify is an enterprise product experience management platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B platform used by large brands to manage product content across retail channels and has no consumer-facing surface that would attract phishing or scam infrastructure.

Saly.history: Saly is a Polish enterprise B2B ecommerce platform built on PHP and Svelte, designed for manufacturers, wholesalers, and distributors. It is a substantial regional product with no notable ownership events.
Saly.summary_abuse: Saly is a Polish B2B ecommerce platform with no notable abuse signal. It targets enterprise manufacturers and distributors and has no documented history of exploitation.

Sameday.history: Sameday is a post-2020 AI-powered automated phone answering platform targeted at home service businesses such as plumbers and HVAC companies. Very limited public founding history is available.
Sameday.summary_abuse: Sameday is an AI phone answering service for home services companies with no documented abuse history. It is a B2B communications tool and does not expose a public hosting surface.

Samsung Food.history: Samsung Food (formerly Whisk) is a recipe saving, meal planning, and grocery management app. Samsung acquired Whisk in 2019 and rebranded it as Samsung Food in 2023.
Samsung Food.summary_abuse: Samsung Food is a consumer recipe and meal planning app owned by Samsung. No notable abuse signal documented.

Sana Commerce.history: Sana Commerce is a Dutch B2B ecommerce platform founded in 2009, specializing in integration with SAP and Microsoft Dynamics ERP systems for wholesale and manufacturing businesses.
Sana Commerce.summary_abuse: Sana Commerce is a B2B ecommerce platform integrated with enterprise ERP systems. No notable abuse signal documented.

Sanity.history: Sanity is a Norwegian headless CMS company founded in 2017 by Even Westvang and others. It offers a real-time hosted structured content platform with an open-source studio editor, and has become a popular choice for developers needing flexible content APIs.
Sanity.summary_abuse: Sanity is a structured content platform and headless CMS with no notable abuse signal. It serves developers and content teams building legitimate websites and applications.

Sanity.io.history: Sanity.io is the same product as Sanity - a Norwegian headless CMS company founded in 2017. The .io domain was the original domain before sanity.io became the primary brand. Same product, same company.
Sanity.io.summary_abuse: Sanity.io is a structured content platform and headless CMS with no notable abuse signal.

SAP.history: SAP SE was founded in 1972 in Walldorf, Germany by five former IBM engineers (Dietmar Hopp, Hasso Plattner, et al.) as Systemanalyse Programmentwicklung. It became the world's leading enterprise application software vendor, going public in 1998. Key acquisitions include Sybase (2010), Concur (2014, $8.3B), Qualtrics (2018, $8B, later partially spun off), and Signavio (2021).
SAP.summary_abuse: SAP is a foundational enterprise software platform with no notable direct abuse signal. As a backend enterprise system handling critical business data, it is a target for sophisticated attacks (e.g., SAP vulnerabilities are occasionally exploited by APT groups), but these reflect security vulnerabilities rather than platform-level abuse.

SAP Commerce Cloud.history: SAP Commerce Cloud originated as Hybris founded 1997 in Germany. SAP acquired Hybris in 2013 and rebranded it as SAP Commerce Cloud.
SAP Commerce Cloud.summary_abuse: SAP Commerce Cloud is an enterprise ecommerce platform with no notable abuse signal. Enterprise procurement gates prevent unauthorized use.

SAP Customer Data Cloud Sign-in.history: Originally Gigya, a customer identity and access management platform founded in 2006 in Israel. SAP acquired Gigya in 2017 for approximately $350 million and rebranded it as SAP Customer Data Cloud.
SAP Customer Data Cloud Sign-in.summary_abuse: SAP Customer Data Cloud handles customer identity and authentication. It has a limited abuse surface as an enterprise-only CIAM solution, though CIAM platforms can be targeted for credential stuffing attacks against hosted login forms. No notable documented abuse incidents.

SAP Upscale Commerce.history: SAP Upscale Commerce was a SaaS ecommerce solution from SAP targeting SMB direct-to-consumer brands. It was developed as part of SAP Customer Experience portfolio and positioned as a lighter-weight alternative to SAP Commerce Cloud for smaller organizations.
SAP Upscale Commerce.summary_abuse: SAP Upscale Commerce is a SaaS ecommerce platform with no notable abuse signal. It targets legitimate SMB merchants and requires SAP account procurement.

Sapo.history: Sapo is a Portuguese multi-channel ecommerce and sales management platform founded around 2008, popular in Portugal and Brazil for online retail management.
Sapo.summary_abuse: Sapo is a regional ecommerce platform primarily used in Portuguese-speaking markets. Like all ecommerce platforms, it could be used to set up fraudulent stores, but no specific documented abuse incidents are known.

Sapper.history: Sapper was a Svelte-based application framework for building server-rendered web apps, created by Rich Harris in 2019 as the precursor to SvelteKit. It was deprecated in 2021 when SvelteKit officially replaced it as the recommended Svelte framework.
Sapper.summary_abuse: Sapper is a deprecated Svelte web framework with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

Sapren.history: Sapren is a Laravel/PHP/MySQL-based CMS. It is a niche content management system with limited public information.
Sapren.summary_abuse: Sapren is a CMS with no notable abuse signal. It is a content management tool with no documented history of exploitation.

Sardine.history: Sardine was founded in 2020 by Soups Ranjan and Aditya Goel, both formerly of Coinbase, to build a fraud prevention and compliance platform targeting fintech and financial services. The company raised $70M in a Series C round in 2025 led by Activant Capital with backing from Andreessen Horowitz, Google Ventures, and others, bringing total funding to $145M.
Sardine.summary_abuse: Sardine is itself an anti-fraud platform rather than a vector for abuse. It processes transaction signals and device fingerprints on behalf of financial institutions, which means it handles sensitive behavioral and payment data, but its exposure is to clients who are themselves regulated entities. There is no documented pattern of Sardine being used for malicious purposes; its consumer-facing surface is negligible and its customer base is enterprise fintechs and banks.

Sardius Media.history: Founded circa 2013 in Kansas City by Jason Shore, Ari Burt, and Mike Read as a broadcast SaaS platform serving churches and mission-driven organizations. No notable ownership changes, forks, or deprecation events since.
Sardius Media.summary_abuse: Sardius Media is a broadcast SaaS platform for mission-driven organizations with no notable abuse signal. It requires paid contracts with demonstrated organisational need and offers no free tier, anonymous signup, or consumer-facing self-service that fraudsters typically exploit. The platform serves a narrow niche with custom-domain hosting limited to paying broadcast customers.

Sare.history: Founded around 2005 in Poland as an email marketing platform. Later became part of Digitree Group S.A., a publicly traded marketing technology group listed on the Warsaw Stock Exchange. Has grown into an omnichannel communication platform supporting email, SMS, web push, and RCS. Reports serving 70% of Polish banks among its clients. No notable acquisitions, forks, or public controversies.
Sare.summary_abuse: SARE is a professional mass-communication platform (email, SMS, RCS) with strong anti-abuse controls. It publishes a detailed anti-spam policy, requires double opt-in consent, verifies sender identity, and prohibits purchased or rented mailing lists. Violations result in account suspension or termination and referral to law enforcement. Its customer base is dominated by regulated entities (banks, insurers, healthcare) that require strict compliance, which limits abuse surface. As with any mass-email platform, the service could theoretically be abused for spam, but active enforcement and KYC requirements keep abuse low relative to legitimate use. A dedicated abuse-reporting channel is available.

Sarka-SPIP.history: Sarka-SPIP is a template skeleton for the French SPIP CMS, created by French developer _Eric_ (Éric Lemoine) and first released around 2006. It evolved through four major branches over 15+ years, tracking SPIP CMS releases. The project is distributed under GPLv3 and has remained an independent community project with no corporate backing or ownership changes.
Sarka-SPIP.summary_abuse: Sarka-SPIP is an open-source template skeleton for the SPIP CMS with no abuse signal. It is a static template layer installed on self-hosted SPIP websites, with no consumer-facing hosting, file-sharing, URL-shortening, or communication platform features. It has no documented abuse history.

Sass.history: Created by Hampton Catlin and released November 28, 2006 as a Ruby-based CSS preprocessor. Originally implemented in Ruby, then gained a C++ implementation (libSass, 2012) and eventually the canonical Dart Sass implementation. The Ruby implementation reached end-of-life in March 2019; libSass was deprecated in October 2020 and reached end-of-life in October 2025. The project is stewarded by the Sass core team with Dart Sass as the primary implementation.
Sass.summary_abuse: Sass is a CSS preprocessor with no notable abuse signal. It compiles to CSS during a build step and has no consumer-facing runtime surface that fraudsters target. It does not offer any web-accessible service, hosting, URL shortening, or content delivery features. As a build-time tool processed entirely on the developer's machine, Sass has no documented history of being used for phishing, malware distribution, or other abusive purposes.

Sassy Social Share.history: First released December 2015 on WordPress.org by the Heateor team. The plugin has been actively maintained with frequent updates, reaching version 3.3.79 and over 100,000 active installations. No ownership changes or deprecation events.
Sassy Social Share.summary_abuse: Sassy Social Share is a WordPress social sharing plugin that does not save cookies and is EU-GDPR compliant. It runs entirely on the user's website without server communication. The plugin itself has no abuse surface, though as with any social sharing widget it exposes buttons linking to third-party platforms. No notable abuse incidents have been documented.

Satori.history: SATORI is a Japanese marketing automation (MA) platform developed by SATORI, Inc. The company provides lead generation, nurturing, and management tools for B2B marketing teams. Holds ISMS (ISO 27001) certification. No notable ownership changes.
Satori.summary_abuse: SATORI is a Japanese B2B marketing automation platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves enterprise marketing teams in Japan for lead management and does not host consumer-facing content or allow anonymous signup. Holds ISMS certification. No documented abuse patterns.

Satori Studio Bento.history: Developed by Andrii Shekhirev (Satori Studio), an Envato Elite author. First published on WordPress.org in February 2017. Last updated September 2020 and effectively abandoned since, with no further maintenance or compatibility updates. No notable ownership changes or controversies.
Satori Studio Bento.summary_abuse: Bento is a niche WordPress theme with only 3,000+ active installations that has not been updated since 2020. While WordPress themes can appear in phishing or scam storefronts, Bento has no documented specific abuse incidents. Its very small install base and abandoned status make it an unlikely target for abuse campaigns.

Satu.history: Satu.kz is a Kazakhstani B2B marketplace for consumer, industrial, and wholesale products. It is one of the leading online marketplace platforms in Kazakhstan.
Satu.summary_abuse: Satu.kz is a Central Asian B2B marketplace with a moderate abuse risk typical of open marketplace platforms. As with all multi-vendor markets, counterfeit goods listings and fraudulent sellers are a recurring concern, particularly in less-regulated jurisdictions. No specific major documented abuse campaigns are associated.

Satūs.history: Satus is an open-source Next.js and React starter template with WebGL integration, created by darkroom.engineering. It is a developer boilerplate project with no notable ownership or deprecation history.
Satūs.summary_abuse: Satus is an open-source frontend starter template with no abuse signal. It is a developer boilerplate with no consumer-facing surface.

Sauce Social Commerce.history: Sauce Social Commerce was developed by SupaDupa Me Limited, founded by Guy Schragger and Yemi Adeleye. The company originally built SupaDupa, a no-code e-commerce platform, before evolving into social commerce tools under the brand Sauce around 2018. The company has investment from Fidelity Investments.
Sauce Social Commerce.summary_abuse: Sauce Social Commerce is a social commerce platform for e-commerce stores, integrating influencer content and UGC for legitimate marketing purposes. It has no free tier for anonymous use and integrates with established e-commerce platforms. No notable abuse incidents are publicly documented.

SavvyCal.history: Founded by Derrick Reimer in early 2020 as a bootstrapped indie SaaS alternative to Calendly. Received funding from the TinySeed accelerator program. Derrick previously co-founded Drip (acquired by Leadpages). The company remained independent with a small team running the product.
SavvyCal.summary_abuse: SavvyCal is a meeting scheduling tool with no notable abuse signal. It requires account registration and offers custom-domain support only on paid plans. The vendor publishes a privacy notice, terms of use, and an acceptable use policy, and can disable accounts for violations. As a relatively small platform (~2,000 customers) in the scheduling space, abuse volume is negligible compared to mainstream competitors.

Say.ac.history: Say.ac is a Turkish web analytics platform launched in 2011 that provides website visitor tracking, analysis, and reporting tools for webmasters. It offers directory listings and social features for the webmaster community. No notable ownership changes or acquisitions are publicly documented.
Say.ac.summary_abuse: Say.ac is a Turkish web analytics platform that provides website visitor tracking scripts for webmasters to embed on their sites. As a visitor tracking tool, it collects browsing data from site visitors. The platform requires user registration and is primarily used for legitimate website analytics by its webmaster community. No documented patterns of significant abuse beyond typical concerns associated with third-party tracking scripts are recorded.

Sazito.history: Sazito appears to be an Iranian-based web platform. Very limited public information is available.
Sazito.summary_abuse: Sazito has very limited public information available for assessment. It appears to be an Iranian web platform with no specific documented abuse history.

SberCRM.history: SberCRM was developed by Sber Business Soft (a subsidiary of Sberbank, Russia's largest bank) to serve the Russian CRM market. The product initially focused on sales management and later expanded into real estate, tender management, and enterprise low-code customization. In April 2026, the parent company was rebranded as Sber2B AI as part of a broader restructuring of Sber's non-financial business services ecosystem. The company holds Russian IT accreditation since March 2022.
SberCRM.summary_abuse: SberCRM is a business CRM platform with no notable abuse signal. It requires business registration (INN/Tax ID) for signup and primarily serves legitimate B2B customers in the Russian market. As a cloud-hosted SaaS CRM running on Russian cloud infrastructure (ООО Облачные технологии), its abuse risk profile is that of a standard business tool. Sberbank's regulatory oversight provides compliance baseline. There are no documented patterns of SberCRM being used for phishing, scams, or fraud.

Scala.history: Designed by Martin Odersky at EPFL in Switzerland, Scala first publicly released on January 20, 2004. Odersky and collaborators launched Typesafe Inc. (later renamed Lightbend) in May 2011 to provide commercial support, training, and services. Scala 3 (codenamed Dotty), a major redesign of the language, was released in 2021.
Scala.summary_abuse: Scala has no notable abuse signal. It is a general-purpose programming language compiled to JVM bytecode, JavaScript, or native code — it has no consumer-facing surface or free-tier hosting features that fraudsters typically target. Abuse is practically nonexistent relative to legitimate enterprise and big-data use.

Scalapay.history: Scalapay was founded in 2019 by Simone Mancini and Johnny Mitrevski in Milan, Italy as a BNPL payment method for European ecommerce. It raised $155M in Series A, then $497M in Series B led by Tencent, becoming Italy's first unicorn. In 2025 the European Investment Bank invested €70M.
Scalapay.summary_abuse: Scalapay is a BNPL payment provider with moderate fraud risk inherent to the buy-now-pay-later sector. BNPL services are targeted by fraudsters using stolen identities to make purchases without repaying; Scalapay employs identity verification and risk scoring to mitigate this. No specific unusual abuse incidents beyond standard BNPL fraud patterns are documented.

Scalar.history: Scalar was founded around 2021 as API Documentation, Inc. and is led by CEO Marc Laventure. The company built an open-source API reference and documentation platform that gained rapid adoption in the API ecosystem, reaching 15,000 GitHub stars and being adopted as the default documentation UI by frameworks like ElysiaJS, Nitro, and Litestar. The product has grown from an API reference renderer into a full platform including an API client, registry, SDK generation, and hosted documentation. No acquisitions or ownership changes have been publicly documented.
Scalar.summary_abuse: Scalar is an API documentation and testing platform with a limited abuse surface. It primarily serves developers creating API references and documentation — it does not host user-generated content, enable anonymous communication, or offer free tier with custom domain hosting that fraudsters typically exploit. The open-source components are self-hosted by users. Scalar publishes a privacy policy, uses Fathom Analytics (privacy-focused), and processes payments through Stripe (PCI-DSS compliant). No notable abuse incidents have been publicly documented.

Scalefast.history: Scalefast was founded in 2014 by Nicolas Stehle and Olivier Schott as an outsourced ecommerce platform for global brands. In May 2022, it was acquired by ESW (eShopWorld), a global DTC ecommerce company.
Scalefast.summary_abuse: Scalefast is an outsourced enterprise ecommerce platform with no notable abuse signal. It operates as a merchant-of-record service for large brands and does not provide open-access hosting for fraudulent storefronts.

ScandiPWA.history: ScandiPWA is an open-source Progressive Web Application theme for Magento 2, developed in React by Scandiweb, a Polish ecommerce development company. It was created to provide a modern PWA frontend alternative for Magento 2 storefronts, leveraging React and GraphQL. The project is maintained by Scandiweb and has gained adoption in the Magento ecommerce community.
ScandiPWA.summary_abuse: ScandiPWA is a frontend theme for Magento 2 with no notable abuse signal. As an open-source PWA theme that must be installed on a merchant's own Magento instance, it has no consumer-facing platform, no user accounts, no content-hosting capabilities, and no anonymous signup. Any abuse would be attributable to the store built on top of it, not to the theme itself.

ScanNet Webshop.history: ScanNet Webshop is a Danish ecommerce solution from ScanNet, a Danish web hosting and internet services company. Limited public founding history is available.
ScanNet Webshop.summary_abuse: ScanNet Webshop is a small Danish ecommerce platform with no documented abuse history. It serves Danish small businesses and has limited exposure to large-scale fraud operations.

ScapBot.history: ScapBot JSC was founded in Hanoi, Vietnam, with a business registration issued March 12, 2024. The company develops LikePion, an SEO backlink automation tool. It also offers SEO services, backlink services, and web design. CEO is Trung Do; the company is a small Vietnamese joint stock company with no notable ownership changes or acquisitions.
ScapBot.summary_abuse: ScapBot's LikePion is an SEO backlink automation tool that creates entity profiles, social backlinks, and automated URL indexing. These features are commonly used in spam SEO and link-building schemes, giving the platform moderate abuse potential. However, onboarding requires contact with sales, and the tool targets legitimate Vietnamese SEO professionals and agencies. The company publishes a privacy policy and terms of service. No major public abuse incidents have been documented. The abuse risk is moderate: the tool's core function is link-building which can be used for black-hat SEO, but its B2B sales model limits casual abuse.

Scayle.history: Founded in 2018 as the internal ecommerce engine powering ABOUT YOU, a European fashion retailer. Later commercialized as a standalone enterprise platform under ABOUT YOU Group. Serves customers including Levi's, Harrods, and Manchester United. Named a Challenger in the 2025 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Digital Commerce.
Scayle.summary_abuse: Scayle is an enterprise B2C ecommerce platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a closed SaaS platform serving established retail brands, with no free tier, no anonymous signup, and no user-generated content hosting. Abuse surface is limited to standard ecommerce fraud risks managed at the merchant level, not via the platform itself.

Sched.history: Founded in 2008 as an event scheduling platform. Has since grown to serve over 30,000 events across conferences, festivals, and professional development. No notable ownership changes, acquisitions, or deprecation events.
Sched.summary_abuse: Sched is an event management SaaS platform with limited abuse surface. Event creation requires organizer verification, and the platform does not offer anonymous signup or consumer-facing hosting features that fraudsters typically exploit. SOC 2 Type II certified with GDPR and FERPA compliance. No notable public incidents of widespread abuse documented.

Schedule Engine.history: Schedule Engine is a contractor scheduling and live chat product developed by ServiceTitan. ServiceTitan was founded in 2007 by Ara Mahdessian and Vahe Kuzoyan and launched its cloud platform in 2012. The Schedule Engine product was later rebranded as Scheduling Pro within the ServiceTitan suite. ServiceTitan went public on Nasdaq (TTAN) in December 2024 with a $614M annual revenue and approximately 2,870 employees. No notable rebranding or deprecation events are associated with Schedule Engine specifically.
Schedule Engine.summary_abuse: Schedule Engine is a contractor scheduling and live chat widget with a low abuse profile. It does not offer anonymous signup, custom-domain content hosting, or mass-communication features that are commonly targeted by fraudsters. The widget allows customers to book service appointments via website embeds and Google integration, which has limited abuse surface area. The vendor, ServiceTitan, is a publicly traded company with a published AUP, responsible disclosure policy, SOC 2 and ISO 27001 certifications, and a security reporting channel at security@servicetitan.com. No notable public abuse incidents have been documented for Schedule Engine specifically.

ScheduleAnyone.history: ScheduleAnyone is a business management and scheduling automation platform. Very limited public founding history is available.
ScheduleAnyone.summary_abuse: ScheduleAnyone is a niche business scheduling platform with no documented abuse history.

Schema App.history: Hunch Manifest was founded in 2011 by Mark van Berkel to build semantic technology products. Schema App officially launched in 2016 as an enterprise schema markup platform, co-founded by Mark and Martha van Berkel. The company remains independent and privately held as Hunch Manifest Inc, based in Guelph, Ontario, Canada. No notable ownership changes, acquisitions, or deprecation events since launch.
Schema App.summary_abuse: Schema App has minimal abuse signal. It is an enterprise SaaS platform for generating schema markup and managing knowledge graphs, deployed server-side on customer websites. It has no free tier, no anonymous signup, and does not host user-generated content under custom domains. The platform serves regulated enterprise customers including financial and healthcare institutions, requiring contracts and onboarding. SOC 2 Type II compliance indicates operational security controls. No documented abuse incidents or phishing/scam associations are publicly known.

SchemaPlus.history: Founded 2018 as SchemaPlus Inc in New York, NY. Launched on the Shopify App Store as a schema markup tool for Shopify stores. No notable ownership changes or deprecation events.
SchemaPlus.summary_abuse: SchemaPlus is a Shopify app for generating structured data markup. It has no notable abuse signal — it is a backend SEO tool that operates within Shopify's API sandbox, does not host user content, does not offer custom domains, and does not enable anonymous signups. There are no documented abuse patterns associated with this service.

School Kiwi.history: School Kiwi is a New Zealand-based student management system by KAMAR, focused on administrative automation for schools. Limited public founding history is available.
School Kiwi.summary_abuse: School Kiwi is a school management system with no notable abuse signal. It serves educational institutions with student records management and does not expose consumer-facing public hosting.

Scientific Linux.history: Scientific Linux was created around 2004 by Fermi National Accelerator Laboratory (Fermilab) and CERN as a free Enterprise Linux rebuild for scientific computing, based on Red Hat Enterprise Linux. In December 2022, Fermilab and CERN recommended migrating to alternative distributions, and Scientific Linux reached official end of life on June 30, 2024.
Scientific Linux.summary_abuse: Scientific Linux is a server operating system with no consumer-facing surface, free tier, or custom-domain hosting features that would attract abusers. As a backend OS rebuild used primarily in institutional scientific computing environments, it has no notable abuse signal or documented pattern of exploitation for phishing, scams, or malware hosting.

Scissor Themes Writee.history: Writee is a free WordPress blog theme by Scissor Themes, designed for personal, food, travel, and corporate blogs. It is a free GPL-licensed theme with no notable ownership events.
Scissor Themes Writee.summary_abuse: Writee is a free WordPress theme with no notable abuse signal. It is a standard GPL theme with no specific documented exploitation patterns.

Scoop.it.history: Scoop.it was founded in 2007 as Goojet by Guillaume Decugis and Marc Rougier, then relaunched as a content curation service in 2011. In October 2018, it was acquired by Linkfluence, a social media intelligence firm.
Scoop.it.summary_abuse: Scoop.it is a content curation platform with modest abuse risk. Its public topic pages could be used to amplify spam or misleading content, but no specific large-scale abuse campaigns are documented and the platform is primarily used by legitimate content marketers.

ScoreApp.history: ScoreApp is a quiz marketing and lead generation platform founded around 2021 in the UK. It provides interactive quiz funnels for businesses to capture and qualify leads.
ScoreApp.summary_abuse: ScoreApp is a quiz marketing platform for lead generation with modest abuse risk. As with any interactive form platform, quiz funnels could be used to phish for personal data. No specific documented abuse campaigns are associated with ScoreApp.

Scorpion.history: Founded in 2001 by Rustin Kretz in Valencia, California, Scorpion is a digital marketing and technology platform focused on legal, healthcare, home services, and franchise industries. The company raised $100 million in private equity from Bregal Sagemount in April 2021 to support its expansion.
Scorpion.summary_abuse: Scorpion is a B2B digital marketing services platform aimed at local businesses, with no notable abuse signal as a tool used by bad actors. Its consumer surface is primarily internal dashboards for paying clients rather than public-facing user-generated content.

Screenfull.js.history: Screenfull.js was created by Sindre Sorhus, a prominent open-source developer, as a simple cross-browser wrapper for the JavaScript Fullscreen API. Released under the MIT license, it provides a unified API for entering and exiting fullscreen mode across different browsers. It has become a widely used utility in web development projects.
Screenfull.js.summary_abuse: Screenfull.js has no notable abuse signal. It is a small open-source JavaScript library that wraps the browser Fullscreen API. It has no network functionality, no user accounts, no content-hosting capabilities, and no server-side component. Any involvement in malicious activity would be attributable to the website embedding it, not to the library itself.

Screets.history: Screets is a small software vendor based in Sarajevo/Turkey that developed a one-time-purchase chat widget for WordPress, HTML, and PHP sites, active since around 2007; no notable ownership changes or significant industry events.
Screets.summary_abuse: Screets is a small chat widget with no notable abuse signal. It is self-hosted on client websites and does not expose free-tier hosting of content under custom domains that would attract phishing actors.

script.aculo.us.history: script.aculo.us is a JavaScript UI library created by Thomas Fuchs in 2005, providing animations and UI widgets. It was widely used in the Web 2.0 era but has been largely abandoned as jQuery and modern frameworks superseded it.
script.aculo.us.summary_abuse: script.aculo.us has no notable abuse signal; it is an older client-side JavaScript UI library.

Scrivito.history: Scrivito is a headless CMS product by JustRelate Group GmbH (formerly Infopark AG), a German company founded circa 1995 with 30+ years of digital experience. The Scrivito product was developed as a decoupled/React-based CMS and later rebranded as JustRelate Web Builder. The company has offices in Berlin (HQ), Hamburg, Munich, Paris, Lille, and Wroclaw. Customers include Lacoste, L'Oreal, Nestle, Siemens Healthineers, and other European enterprises. No notable ownership changes or acquisitions documented.
Scrivito.summary_abuse: Scrivito/JustRelate Web Builder is a B2B enterprise headless CMS with low abuse signal. The platform serves enterprise clients in Europe including government, healthcare, and retail sectors. Onboarding involves enterprise contracts with KYC, making casual abuse unattractive. The company is GDPR-compliant and offers a status page for service transparency. No notable public incidents of platform abuse have been documented. As a managed CMS with enterprise sales, the risk of mass phishing or scam hosting is minimal.

ScrollMagic.history: Created by Jan Paepke and first released around 2014 as a jQuery plugin for scroll-based animations. Widely adopted for parallax and scroll-triggered effects. Between 2025 and 2026 the library was rewritten as ScrollMagic v3 with zero dependencies, native TypeScript support, and an IntersectionObserver-based architecture, removing the jQuery and GSAP dependencies of v2. No ownership changes or acquisitions.
ScrollMagic.summary_abuse: ScrollMagic is a client-side JavaScript library for scroll-driven interactions with no server-side component, no user data collection, and no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters target. It has no abuse signal.

scrollreveal.history: ScrollReveal is a JavaScript library for animating HTML elements as they enter or leave the browser viewport. Created by Julian Lloyd, it is a lightweight animation utility.
scrollreveal.summary_abuse: scrollreveal has no notable abuse signal; it is a client-side scroll animation library.

Scully.history: Created by HeroDevs (Aaron Frost and team) around 2019 as a static site generator for Angular applications embracing the Jamstack. Last release v2.1.41 was in September 2022; the project has been dormant since with no commits or releases in over three years.
Scully.summary_abuse: Scully is a build-time static site generator with no consumer-facing hosting or user-content surface. It has no documented abuse signal.

SDL Tridion.history: SDL Tridion is an enterprise CMS originally developed by Tridion B.V. in the Netherlands. SDL acquired Tridion in 2007. SDL was later acquired by RWS Group in 2020, and the product continues under the RWS brand as Tridion.
SDL Tridion.summary_abuse: SDL Tridion is an enterprise CMS with no notable abuse signal. It is deployed on-premises or in private cloud for large enterprises and is not publicly accessible for unauthorized use.

Seal Subscriptions.history: Seal Subscriptions is a European-built Shopify subscriptions app that has served Shopify merchants for over eight years; it has grown to 40,000+ customers and processed over $100 billion in renewals, with no notable ownership changes.
Seal Subscriptions.summary_abuse: Seal Subscriptions is a Shopify subscription billing tool with no notable abuse signal. It operates as a backend billing layer for legitimate merchants and does not expose public-facing hosted content or free anonymous accounts that would attract fraudulent actors.

SealMetrics.history: SealMetrics was founded in 2021 by Rafael Jimenez in Barcelona, Spain, as a cookieless analytics platform built to comply with GDPR and privacy regulations without requiring user consent banners.
SealMetrics.summary_abuse: SealMetrics has no notable abuse signal. It is a cookieless web analytics tool focused on privacy compliance, with no consumer-facing features that would attract fraudsters or scammers.

SeamlessCMS.history: SeamlessCMS was founded in 1999 and is based in Melbourne, Australia, providing CMS solutions for local government agencies with approximately 500 government websites on its platform; no notable ownership changes are recorded.
SeamlessCMS.summary_abuse: SeamlessCMS is a niche government CMS with no notable abuse signal; it serves local government agencies and does not expose public free-tier user-generated content hosting.

Search Magic.history: Search Magic is an ecommerce site-search SaaS product offered by Your Store Wizards, targeting Shopify and similar online stores; no notable ownership changes or major events documented.
Search Magic.summary_abuse: Search Magic is a niche ecommerce search tool embedded on merchant storefronts. It has no consumer-facing surface that scammers would directly target and no documented abuse history.

Searchanise.history: Searchanise was launched around 2014 as a SaaS search and filter solution targeting ecommerce platforms including Shopify, WooCommerce, and BigCommerce; it remains an independent product with no notable ownership changes.
Searchanise.summary_abuse: Searchanise is an ecommerce search widget with no documented abuse history. It operates within merchant storefronts and has no consumer-facing attack surface that bad actors would preferentially target.

SearchFit.history: SearchFit is an Australian ecommerce platform targeting mid-market and enterprise retailers; it provides hosted ecommerce websites with search and product management features. No significant acquisition or rebranding events are publicly documented.
SearchFit.summary_abuse: SearchFit is a B2B ecommerce platform for established retailers with no notable abuse signal; it does not offer free public hosting or anonymous signups that attract fraudulent storefronts.

Searchine.history: Searchine is a site search analytics SaaS providing insights into visitor search behavior. It is a small product with limited public history.
Searchine.summary_abuse: Searchine is a site search analytics tool with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B website enhancement product with no documented history of exploitation.

SearchiQ.history: SearchiQ is a cloud-based site search SaaS that integrates with websites to provide fast, customizable search; it launched with a freemium model and serves small to mid-size websites. No notable ownership changes or significant industry events are documented.
SearchiQ.summary_abuse: SearchiQ is a site-search widget with no notable abuse signal; it is an embedded search backend tool with no public user-generated content hosting.

Searchspring.history: Searchspring was founded in 2007 by Gareth Dismore and Scott Zielinski as a bootstrapped e-commerce search and merchandising platform. In 2019 it was acquired by venture equity firm Scaleworks, and later received a strategic growth investment from PSG in 2022.
Searchspring.summary_abuse: Searchspring has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B e-commerce search and merchandising platform used by brands and retailers, with no consumer-facing or content-hosting features that would attract fraudsters.

Searchtap.history: Searchtap is an Indian search-as-a-service platform primarily targeting Shopify merchants; it offers fast, hosted search functionality with no notable ownership changes documented.
Searchtap.summary_abuse: Searchtap is a B2B hosted search tool for ecommerce stores with no notable abuse signal; it operates as a backend indexing and search service without public user-generated content exposure.

Seated.history: Seated is a live-event ticketing platform for venues and artists; it launched to serve direct-to-fan ticket sales for live events and live streams. No notable ownership changes or significant events are publicly documented.
Seated.summary_abuse: Seated is a live-event ticketing platform with limited abuse signal; it serves artist and venue clients directly rather than anonymous end-users and does not offer free-tier public hosting of content.

Seatics.history: Seatics is a US-based SaaS platform providing interactive venue mapping for arenas, stadiums, and convention centers; it serves ticketing companies and venues. No notable ownership changes or significant events are publicly documented.
Seatics.summary_abuse: Seatics is a B2B venue mapping service with no notable abuse signal; it serves established venue and ticketing clients and does not expose public user-generated content.

Secomapp.history: Secomapp is a Vietnamese Shopify app development company providing multiple Shopify apps including referral, reward, and promotional tools; it has served the Shopify ecosystem for over a decade with no notable ownership changes.
Secomapp.summary_abuse: Secomapp is a Shopify app provider with no notable abuse signal; its apps serve legitimate merchants on the Shopify platform and do not expose public free-form hosting.

Sectigo.history: Sectigo was founded in 1998 as Comodo CA, one of the world's largest SSL/TLS certificate authorities. In 2018, the certificate authority business was rebranded from Comodo CA to Sectigo after being spun off from Comodo Group. GI Partners later announced plans to acquire Sectigo. The company also acquired SiteLock and European partners SSL247 and Xolphin.
Sectigo.summary_abuse: Sectigo has minimal abuse signal for the company itself; however, as a certificate authority, rogue SSL certificates have occasionally been issued under earlier Comodo branding, which were later revoked. The company operates CA/Browser Forum-compliant processes and responds to certificate mis-issuance reports. Legitimate use overwhelmingly dominates.

Section.io.history: Section.io is an Australian CDN and edge-compute platform that allows developers to run reverse proxies and caching modules at the edge, founded around 2012; it has positioned itself as a developer-centric alternative to major CDNs with no notable ownership changes.
Section.io.summary_abuse: Section.io is a CDN with a comparable abuse profile to other content delivery networks; legitimate use overwhelmingly dominates, and the platform serves a developer-oriented audience rather than anonymous public content hosts.

Sections.design Shopify App Optimization.history: Sections.design App Optimization is a small open-source Shopify Liquid snippet by developer Mircea Piturca, designed to defer or control how Shopify apps load to improve store performance; it lives on GitHub with no commercial backing.
Sections.design Shopify App Optimization.summary_abuse: This is an open-source Shopify Liquid snippet with no notable abuse signal; it is a small performance utility with no consumer-facing surface.

SecureMetrics.history: SecurityMetrics (branded as SecureMetrics in Wappalyzer) is a US-based website security and PCI compliance assessment platform. The company has been providing security testing and vulnerability assessments to businesses.
SecureMetrics.summary_abuse: SecureMetrics has no notable abuse signal. It is a security assessment and compliance platform used by businesses to identify vulnerabilities, with no consumer-facing content hosting features.

Securiti.history: Securiti was founded in 2018 and emerged from stealth in 2019 with $31M in Series A funding. It raised $50M (Series B, 2020) and $75M (Series C, 2022) from Mayfield, General Catalyst and Workday. In October 2025, Securiti was acquired by Veeam for $1.725B.
Securiti.summary_abuse: Securiti has no notable abuse signal. It is an enterprise data privacy and security platform serving large organizations, with no consumer-facing or content-hosting features that would attract fraudsters.

Sedna System.history: Sedna System is a real-time yacht management SaaS for monitoring operations, tracking vessel status, and optimizing onboard resources. It is a specialized maritime technology product.
Sedna System.summary_abuse: Sedna System is a yacht management platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a specialized B2B maritime product with no documented history of exploitation.

Seeda.history: Seeda is an AI-powered analytics platform for ecommerce growth. It is a recent AI-augmented product with limited public history.
Seeda.summary_abuse: Seeda is an ecommerce analytics platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a merchant-facing analytics tool with no documented history of exploitation.

SeedGrow.history: SeedGrow is a Shopify marketing solutions app providing customer acquisition and sales optimization tools. It is a small, recent Shopify add-on.
SeedGrow.summary_abuse: SeedGrow is a Shopify marketing app with no notable abuse signal. It is a merchant-facing tool with no documented history of exploitation.

SeedProd Coming Soon.history: SeedProd was founded in 2013 as a WordPress coming-soon page plugin; it later expanded into a full-featured WordPress page builder and landing-page platform while maintaining its original coming-soon/maintenance-mode product line.
SeedProd Coming Soon.summary_abuse: SeedProd is a WordPress coming-soon and page-builder plugin with modest abuse exposure; like other page builders, it can be used to create placeholder or holding pages before a legitimate site launches. It is not notably exploited for phishing or scam storefronts compared to hosted page-builder services.

Seel.history: Seel is a US-based ecommerce returns protection platform that offers merchants revenue protection by providing coverage for returns and refunds; it launched around 2020. No notable ownership changes are documented.
Seel.summary_abuse: Seel is a B2B ecommerce returns-protection service with no notable abuse signal; it is a merchant-facing backend service that does not expose public user content hosting.

Seers.history: Seers is a UK-based privacy and consent management platform founded around 2018, helping businesses comply with GDPR, CCPA, and related privacy regulations.
Seers.summary_abuse: Seers has no notable abuse signal. It is a privacy compliance and consent management tool serving legitimate business compliance needs.

Segmanta.history: Segmanta is an Israeli mobile-first survey and research platform designed for product feedback and brand research; it launched around 2014 and serves enterprise brands and research agencies. No notable ownership changes are documented.
Segmanta.summary_abuse: Segmanta is a B2B survey platform with no notable abuse signal; it serves enterprise clients for brand research and product feedback and does not expose public-facing free content hosting to anonymous users.

SegMate.history: SegMate is a Facebook Messenger bot and marketing automation platform for customer engagement. It operates in the chatbot marketing space which became popular around 2018-2020.
SegMate.summary_abuse: SegMate is a Messenger bot marketing platform with moderate abuse potential. Messenger bot platforms have been used for spam and unsolicited marketing. Meta imposes restrictions on automated messaging, but smaller platforms in this space may be used to circumvent those limits. No specific high-profile incidents are documented for SegMate.

Segment.history: Segment was founded in 2011 by Peter Reinhardt, Ilya Volodarsky, Calvin French-Owen, and Ian Storm Taylor to solve customer data fragmentation across SaaS tools. It became the leading customer data platform (CDP) and was acquired by Twilio in November 2020 for approximately $3.2 billion.
Segment.summary_abuse: Segment is a customer data infrastructure platform used by data and engineering teams. As a backend data-routing service it has no direct consumer-facing surface for phishing or scams. Its main data-risk profile is privacy-related: it routes large volumes of behavioral PII to third-party tools. Twilio-Segment publishes a SOC 2 Type 2 report and GDPR DPA, indicating mature compliance posture.

Segment Consent Manager.history: Segment Consent Manager is a feature/tool from Segment (acquired by Twilio in 2020 for ~$3.2B), designed to automate privacy consent workflows and track user privacy preferences in compliance with GDPR and CCPA.
Segment Consent Manager.summary_abuse: Segment Consent Manager has no notable abuse signal. It is a privacy consent management tool that helps businesses respect user data preferences, with no features that would attract fraudsters.

Segmentify.history: Segmentify is a Turkish ecommerce personalisation and search platform founded around 2015, serving online retailers with AI-driven product recommendations, search, and customer data platform features. No notable ownership changes are documented.
Segmentify.summary_abuse: Segmentify is a B2B ecommerce personalisation and analytics platform with no notable abuse signal; it operates as a backend analytics and recommendation layer for legitimate merchant clients.

SegmentStream.history: SegmentStream is an AI-powered marketing analytics platform founded around 2019, focused on helping marketing teams measure campaign attribution and optimize ad spend using machine learning; it is headquartered in the UK with no notable ownership changes.
SegmentStream.summary_abuse: SegmentStream is a marketing analytics platform with no notable abuse signal; it is a B2B analytics tool that processes advertising and conversion data for paying clients without exposing public user-generated content.

Segmetrics.history: Segmetrics is a US-based marketing analytics SaaS that provides audience insights and customer lifetime value analysis for marketers, launched around 2017. No notable ownership changes are documented.
Segmetrics.summary_abuse: Segmetrics is a B2B marketing analytics platform with no notable abuse signal; it processes first-party customer data for paying marketing clients and does not expose public-facing hosted content.

Seko OmniReturns.history: Seko Logistics is a global logistics company that founded its OmniReturns returns-management portal as a SaaS product for ecommerce merchants; Seko was established in 1997 and the OmniReturns portal is a digital extension of its core freight and returns logistics business.
Seko OmniReturns.summary_abuse: Seko OmniReturns is a B2B logistics returns portal with no notable abuse signal; it is an enterprise service for merchants to manage parcel returns and does not expose anonymous public access.

Select2.history: Select2 is an open-source jQuery plugin providing enhanced select box functionality with searching, remote data, and infinite scrolling. It has been widely used in web development since around 2012.
Select2.summary_abuse: Select2 has no notable abuse signal. It is a frontend jQuery UI library with no network-facing or content-hosting surface that fraudsters would target.

Selectize.history: Selectize is an open-source jQuery-based custom select UI control library, first released around 2013. It provides an extensible replacement for HTML select elements.
Selectize.summary_abuse: Selectize has no notable abuse signal. It is a frontend UI library with no network-facing or content-hosting surface.

Seline.history: Seline is a cookieless web and product analytics platform, a recent entrant in the privacy-first analytics space competing with Plausible and Fathom. It launched around 2023.
Seline.summary_abuse: Seline is a privacy-focused cookieless analytics tool with no notable abuse signal. Its design specifically avoids user tracking, making it an unlikely tool for fraudulent data harvesting.

Sellacious.history: Sellacious is an Indian open-source ecommerce and marketplace platform built on Joomla, launched around 2015; it provides integrated POS and multi-vendor marketplace features as a self-hosted extension. No notable ownership changes or deprecation events are documented.
Sellacious.summary_abuse: Sellacious is a self-hosted open-source ecommerce platform with modest abuse exposure; as a self-hostable marketplace platform, it can be deployed by any operator including those with lax moderation, but this risk is comparable to other open-source ecommerce tools.

SellBe.history: SellBe is a Russian ecommerce store builder with a freemium model, targeting the Russian market.
SellBe.summary_abuse: SellBe is a Russian ecommerce platform with moderate abuse potential. Free ecommerce platforms can be used to create fake or fraudulent storefronts, and its Russian origin limits verifiability of data practices and compliance posture. No specific documented abuse incidents are known.

Sellbrite.history: Sellbrite was founded around 2013 as a multi-channel marketplace listing platform. In April 2019, GoDaddy acquired Sellbrite to power its GoDaddy Marketplaces product, enabling small businesses to sell across Amazon, eBay, Etsy, and Walmart from a single platform.
Sellbrite.summary_abuse: Sellbrite has no notable abuse signal. It is a marketplace listing tool used by legitimate small businesses and is now owned by GoDaddy, which has anti-abuse policies and legal compliance infrastructure.

Selldone.history: Selldone was founded in 2018 by Mehrdad Ghasemizadeh as an all-in-one ecommerce operating system aimed at SMBs, using a transaction-based pricing model.
Selldone.summary_abuse: Selldone is a legitimate ecommerce platform with no notable abuse signal. Its transaction-based free tier could theoretically be used for fraudulent storefronts, but there are no documented patterns of abuse.

Sellerdeck.history: Sellerdeck is a UK-based ecommerce platform launched in 1996 originally under the name Actinic; it was rebranded to Sellerdeck in 2011 to better reflect its focus on helping UK small businesses sell online. It continues to operate as a niche hosted ecommerce solution for UK merchants.
Sellerdeck.summary_abuse: Sellerdeck is a UK ecommerce platform with no notable abuse signal; it is a B2B solution for small UK merchants and does not expose public free-tier hosted storefronts to anonymous operators.

SellersCommerce.history: SellersCommerce was founded in 2013 and is based in Rosemead, California. The company focuses on B2B ecommerce for manufacturers, distributors, and retailers in specialised verticals including medical equipment, promotional products, and uniforms, and has facilitated over $10 billion in GMV across 3,700+ stores.
SellersCommerce.summary_abuse: SellersCommerce is a B2B ecommerce platform targeted at wholesale and industrial businesses with no notable abuse signal. Its enterprise-focused, sales-gated model with complex onboarding reduces the likelihood of anonymous fraudulent storefront creation.

Selless.history: Selless is a small ecommerce SaaS platform with approximately 1,400 live deployments as of 2024; no notable ownership changes or founding details are publicly documented.
Selless.summary_abuse: Selless has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche ecommerce platform with a small user base, and there are no documented instances of it being used for fraudulent storefronts or other abuse.

Sellful.history: Sellful is an AI-powered white-label website builder and business management platform designed for agencies and entrepreneurs, built on top of WooCommerce. It is sold primarily as a lifetime-deal SaaS through platforms like AppSumo and Entrepreneur marketplace.
Sellful.summary_abuse: Sellful is a white-label website builder that allows resellers to create branded storefronts. The white-label resell model with no-code site creation could lower the barrier to fraudulent site creation, though there are no specific documented abuse incidents. The platform's use of WooCommerce as its underlying engine means abuse characteristics are partly inherited from that ecosystem.

Sellfy.history: Sellfy was founded in 2011 in Latvia as a platform for creators to sell digital products such as music, ebooks, videos, and photos. It later expanded to include physical products and subscriptions.
Sellfy.summary_abuse: Sellfy has modest abuse risk as a digital product sales platform. Platforms enabling file-based product delivery can occasionally be used to sell pirated or fraudulent digital content. The platform requires account creation and has payment verification, which limits purely anonymous abuse, but piracy-adjacent stores have been observed.

Selligent.history: Selligent was founded around 1990 in Belgium as an enterprise marketing automation platform. In 2020, it was acquired by CM Group (later rebranded Marigold). In 2025, Zeta Global signed an agreement to acquire Marigold's enterprise software business including Selligent.
Selligent.summary_abuse: Selligent is a marketing automation platform primarily used for mass email and multi-channel campaigns. Like all bulk email platforms, it carries inherent spam risk; its responsible disclosure processes and AUP provisions apply, but bulk email tools broadly can be misused. No specific major abuse incidents are publicly documented for Selligent itself.

Sellingo.history: Sellingo is a Polish ecommerce SaaS platform focused on the Polish market, offering online store creation with a freemium pricing model. No notable ownership changes or founding details are publicly documented in English-language sources.
Sellingo.summary_abuse: Sellingo has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche Polish ecommerce platform with primarily SMB users and no documented patterns of fraudulent storefront abuse.

Sellix.history: Sellix was founded around 2018 in Italy as a cryptocurrency-friendly digital goods ecommerce platform. It accepts PayPal, PerfectMoney, and major cryptocurrencies, targeting indie sellers of digital products.
Sellix.summary_abuse: Sellix is documented as a platform used to sell digital goods including cracked accounts, stolen data, and other illicit items. Its acceptance of cryptocurrency payments and low KYC requirements make it attractive for fraudulent vendors. Multiple threat intelligence reports have identified Sellix storefronts hosting illegitimate digital goods. The platform has an AUP but enforcement has been inconsistent according to security researchers.

SellSite.history: SellSite is a subscription-based online store builder with a usage-based pricing component. It is a small, recent product with limited public history.
SellSite.summary_abuse: SellSite is an online store builder with minor abuse potential. Store creation platforms can be used for fraudulent storefronts, but there is no specific documented abuse history for SellSite.

Sellsy.history: Sellsy is a French cloud-based sales management and CRM platform founded around 2009, targeting small and midsize businesses.
Sellsy.summary_abuse: Sellsy has no notable abuse signal. It is a French CRM and sales management platform used by SMBs with no consumer-facing content hosting features.

Sellwild.history: Sellwild is a marketplace platform designed to connect buyers and sellers; no founding details or notable ownership events are publicly documented.
Sellwild.summary_abuse: Sellwild is a consumer marketplace platform. Like other peer-to-peer marketplaces, it carries some risk of fraudulent listings, but there are no specific documented abuse incidents or patterns that distinguish it from typical low-usage marketplace services.

Selly.history: Selly is a digital goods e-commerce platform, similar to Sellix, that allows sellers to automate digital product delivery. It targets small digital goods sellers and has been active since around 2016.
Selly.summary_abuse: Selly has moderate abuse risk as a digital goods marketplace with cryptocurrency payment support and relatively low KYC requirements. Digital goods platforms of this type are frequently used to sell pirated software, stolen accounts, and other illicit digital goods. No specific Operation Talent-level enforcement is documented for Selly itself.

Selzy.history: Selzy (originally UniSender) is a Russian/Eastern European email marketing platform that launched around 2010 and has expanded its international reach under the Selzy brand. It offers bulk email, A/B testing, and campaign automation with a freemium model.
Selzy.summary_abuse: Selzy is a bulk email marketing platform with elevated abuse risk relative to standard SaaS; its free tier and bulk email capability make it attractive to spammers. Email marketing platforms generally face ongoing challenges with unwanted bulk-email senders, and Selzy's freemium access lowers the barrier for abuse. The vendor publishes an AUP but its enforcement reputation among researchers is not well documented.

Semantic UI.history: Semantic UI was created by Jack Lukic and released around 2013 as an open-source CSS/JS framework. Development has largely stalled on the original project, leading to the Fomantic UI community fork which is more actively maintained.
Semantic UI.summary_abuse: Semantic UI has no notable abuse signal. It is a frontend CSS/JS framework with no network-facing or content-hosting surface.

Sematext Experience.history: Sematext was founded around 2010 and provides observability tools including real-user monitoring (Experience), log management, and infrastructure monitoring. No notable ownership changes have occurred.
Sematext Experience.summary_abuse: Sematext Experience has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B real-user monitoring and observability platform with no consumer-facing features.

Semplice.history: Semplice is a WordPress-based website builder designed for designers and creative portfolios, founded around 2014.
Semplice.summary_abuse: Semplice has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche WordPress portfolio builder aimed at designers with no consumer-facing content hosting features.

SEMrush.history: SEMrush was founded in 2008 in Saint Petersburg, Russia by Oleg Shchegolev and Dmitry Melnikov. The company relocated its headquarters to Boston, USA, and went public on NYSE in 2021 under the ticker SEMR.
SEMrush.summary_abuse: SEMrush is a mainstream SEO and digital marketing analytics platform with minimal abuse signal. Competitors and SEO specialists occasionally use it for competitive intelligence scraping, but the platform itself is not a vehicle for fraud or phishing.
