Slice.summary_abuse: Slice has a minimal abuse risk. It is a food ordering platform for independent pizzerias, where most abuse risk relates to fraudulent orders or payment chargebacks, common to any food delivery platform. No platform-specific abuse pattern has been documented.

Slick.history: Slick is a jQuery carousel plugin created by Ken Wheeler, released around 2013. Once one of the most popular slider libraries with over 28,000 GitHub stars, the original author stopped active development around 2016-2017. The repository has accumulated over 1,000 open issues and remains effectively abandoned.
Slick.summary_abuse: Slick has no abuse signal. It is a client-side JavaScript carousel library with no server-side surface and no consumer-facing features that fraudsters could exploit.

SlickStack.history: SlickStack was created by Jesse Nickles at LittleBizzy, initially as a collection of private scripts for their managed WordPress hosting, then open-sourced on GitHub around 2018. It is a Bash-only LEMP stack automation script targeting WordPress on Ubuntu LTS, with no external language dependencies.
SlickStack.summary_abuse: SlickStack is a backend server-provisioning tool with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target. It automates WordPress server setup on private infrastructure, so abuse risk is minimal. No documented abuse patterns or incidents.

SlickText.history: SlickText was founded in 2012 in Jamestown, New York as an SMS marketing platform for businesses of all sizes.
SlickText.summary_abuse: SlickText is an SMS marketing platform. Like all mass-text services, it can be misused to send unsolicited spam or smishing (SMS phishing) messages. The company publishes an AUP and implements opt-in verification requirements, but SMS marketing platforms broadly carry moderate abuse risk due to their mass-messaging capabilities.

Slidebean.history: Slidebean was founded in 2014 in San Jose, Costa Rica as an AI-powered pitch deck and presentation creation tool, backed by Y Combinator and growing to serve startup founders and entrepreneurs.
Slidebean.summary_abuse: Slidebean has no notable abuse signal. It is a pitch deck creation tool used by legitimate entrepreneurs and startup founders.

slideout.history: slideout is a JavaScript library for creating touch slideout navigation menus, created around 2015. The project is largely abandoned.
slideout.summary_abuse: slideout has no notable abuse signal; it is a small client-side navigation plugin.

Slider Captcha.history: Slider Captcha is an open-source client-side captcha library hosted on GitHub, implementing a drag-slider verification mechanism to protect web forms from spam.
Slider Captcha.summary_abuse: Slider Captcha is an anti-abuse tool designed to protect web forms from spam. It has no abuse signal of its own.

Slider Revolution.history: Slider Revolution is a premium WordPress and HTML5 slider plugin created around 2012 by ThemePunch, becoming one of the most widely used slider plugins in the WordPress ecosystem with millions of installs.
Slider Revolution.summary_abuse: Slider Revolution has no notable abuse signal as a software product. As a widely-used WordPress plugin, its past security vulnerabilities (most notably a critical file upload vulnerability in 2014) were exploited to compromise WordPress sites, but the plugin itself is a legitimate tool.

Slim Select.history: Slim Select is a small JavaScript library for enhanced select dropdowns, maintained as an open-source project.
Slim Select.summary_abuse: Slim Select has no abuse signal. It is a client-side UI component library with no server-side surface.

Slim SEO.history: Slim SEO is a lightweight WordPress SEO plugin created by MetaBox team in Vietnam, released as a minimal-configuration alternative to heavier plugins like Yoast SEO.
Slim SEO.summary_abuse: Slim SEO has no abuse signal. It is a WordPress SEO plugin with no consumer-facing surface.

Slimbox.history: Slimbox was created around 2007 by Christophe Beyls (digitalia.be) as a lightweight MooTools-based lightbox clone of Lightbox 2. It was a popular jQuery/MooTools-era image overlay library that is now effectively abandoned as MooTools fell out of mainstream use.
Slimbox.summary_abuse: Slimbox is an abandoned MooTools JavaScript lightbox library. It has no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse history.

Slimbox 2.history: Slimbox 2 is an updated version of Slimbox, a lightweight jQuery-free lightbox plugin from digitalia.be. It is not actively maintained and is considered legacy software.
Slimbox 2.summary_abuse: Slimbox 2 has no abuse signal. It is a legacy client-side JavaScript lightbox library with no server-side surface.

Slingshot.history: Slingshot is a project management and analytics platform that combines real-time data analytics with collaboration features for data-driven teams.
Slingshot.summary_abuse: Slingshot has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B project management and analytics SaaS with no consumer-facing hosting surface that fraudsters target.

Slotti.history: Slotti is a Finnish cloud-based appointment booking and payment system for service businesses, targeting small and medium service providers.
Slotti.summary_abuse: Slotti has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche appointment booking SaaS for service businesses in Finland with no consumer-facing hosting surface fraudsters target.

SM Sold.history: SM Sold is a real estate technology platform providing listing and transaction management for agents and brokers. Very limited public founding history is available.
SM Sold.summary_abuse: SM Sold is a real estate management platform with no documented abuse history. It serves licensed real estate professionals rather than general public users.

Smaily.history: Smaily was founded in 2006 by Erkki Markus in Tallinn, Estonia, initially as a one-person operation serving Baltic businesses when email marketing was rare in the region. In 2011, advertising industry veteran Tanel Rand joined as co-founder, and the company grew to serve over 20,000 customers with a team of 18. The platform later absorbed the Mailbow brand. It has remained independent and is the market leader in email marketing for Estonia and the Baltic region.
Smaily.summary_abuse: Smaily is an email marketing SaaS based in Estonia, serving primarily Baltic and Nordic businesses. As with any bulk email platform, Smaily carries some risk of being used for spam if signup controls are weak. However, the company operates in the EU under GDPR and enforces an anti-spam policy. No documented large-scale spam campaigns or phishing abuse specifically linked to Smaily have been publicly reported.

Smallbox.history: Smallbox is a Canadian web agency and CMS platform based in Edmonton, Alberta, offering a proprietary content management system alongside custom web development and digital marketing services.
Smallbox.summary_abuse: Smallbox has no notable abuse signal. It is a small Canadian web agency CMS used for legitimate business websites.

Smallchat.history: Smallchat is a simple live chat tool that routes website visitor messages directly into a Slack channel, created as a lightweight alternative to full-featured chat platforms.
Smallchat.summary_abuse: Smallchat has no notable abuse signal. It is a simple Slack-based chat widget with no content hosting surface that fraudsters would target.

Smart Ad Server.history: Founded in 2001 in France, Smart AdServer became a leading independent ad server and RTB platform for publishers. It rebranded to Equativ in June 2022 after merging with LiquidM (2019) and DynAdmic (2021). Bridgepoint Group acquired a majority stake in 2023, and Equativ merged with SSP Sharethrough in 2024.
Smart Ad Server.summary_abuse: Smart Ad Server (now Equativ) is an enterprise ad serving platform. As an ad-tech company processing significant targeting data, it has inherent privacy risk, but no notable documented fraud or abuse specific to the platform. It is used by legitimate publishers and advertisers.

Smart Analytics.history: Smart Analytics is an AI-powered ecommerce analytics and sales optimization platform. No founding details are documented.
Smart Analytics.summary_abuse: Smart Analytics is an analytics platform with no notable abuse signal.

SMART L-Gov.history: SMART L-Gov is a Japanese government digital services platform developed by Smart Value Co., providing resident-facing information and emergency alert systems for local governments. Limited public history is available.
SMART L-Gov.summary_abuse: SMART L-Gov is a Japanese government services platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves local governments and is not a publicly accessible consumer hosting service.

Smart Slider 3.history: Smart Slider 3 is a WordPress slider plugin developed by Nextend, a Hungarian web agency, launched around 2014 as a SEO-friendly and responsive alternative to other slider plugins.
Smart Slider 3.summary_abuse: Smart Slider 3 has no abuse signal. It is a self-hosted WordPress plugin with no consumer-facing surface.

SmartDX.history: SmartDX is a cookieless tracking alternative that claims to revolutionize user tracking without cookies. No founding details are documented.
SmartDX.summary_abuse: SmartDX is a cookieless tracking technology that collects user behavior data without consent-based cookies. As a tracking technology, it has inherent privacy and potential misuse concerns if deployed without adequate user disclosure.

SmartEmailing.history: SmartEmailing was founded in 2009 by David Kirs and is headquartered in Brno, Czech Republic. The platform provides email marketing automation tools primarily serving the Czech and Slovak market. It has remained independently operated with no notable acquisitions or rebrands.
SmartEmailing.summary_abuse: SmartEmailing is a Czech email marketing and automation SaaS serving primarily Central European businesses. As a bulk email platform, it carries inherent risk of spam misuse, but it operates under EU GDPR and targets professional marketers. No documented large-scale spam incidents or phishing abuse specifically tied to SmartEmailing have been publicly reported.

Smarter Click.history: Smarter Click is a UK-based retargeting and on-site remarketing technology company that provides exit-intent popups and behavioral targeting tools for e-commerce sites.
Smarter Click.summary_abuse: Smarter Click is a retargeting and popup tool. Exit-intent and behavioral popup technologies are associated with dark UX patterns but not with fraud or phishing. No notable abuse incidents are documented.

SmartHint.history: SmartHint is a Brazilian e-commerce product recommendation and search engine SaaS, founded around 2018 to help online retailers improve product discovery and conversions.
SmartHint.summary_abuse: SmartHint has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B product recommendation engine embedded in e-commerce sites with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters target.

Smartling.history: Founded in 2009 by Jack Welde and Andrey Akselrod in New York City as a cloud-based translation management system. Smartling became one of the leading enterprise localization platforms, raising significant VC funding over multiple rounds.
Smartling.summary_abuse: Smartling has no notable abuse signal. It is an enterprise translation management platform used by large companies for content localization workflows.

Smartlook.history: Founded in 2016 in Brno, Czech Republic as a qualitative analytics and session recording platform. Smartlook raised venture funding and was acquired by Cisco in June 2023 to enhance Cisco AppDynamics and full-stack observability offerings.
Smartlook.summary_abuse: Smartlook is a session recording and analytics platform. It captures user interactions on websites and apps, which involves processing behavioral data; GDPR compliance is documented. No notable fraud or abuse incidents specific to Smartlook are documented.

Smartocto.history: Smartocto is a Dutch content analytics platform for news publishers and media organizations, providing real-time data on content performance.
Smartocto.summary_abuse: Smartocto has no notable abuse signal. It is a content analytics tool used by legitimate news publishers and media organizations.

Smartpoint.history: Smartpoint is a car dealer conversion optimization SaaS. No founding details are documented.
Smartpoint.summary_abuse: Smartpoint is an automotive dealer conversion optimization tool with no notable abuse signal.

SmartRecruiters.history: SmartRecruiters was founded in 2010 in San Francisco as a talent acquisition platform. It grew to significant scale serving large enterprises, and in September 2025, SAP completed its acquisition of SmartRecruiters, making it a subsidiary of the German enterprise software giant.
SmartRecruiters.summary_abuse: SmartRecruiters is a B2B HR and recruitment platform with no notable abuse signal. Its enterprise-focused, sales-gated model and identity-verified user base make it an unlikely vector for phishing, spam, or fraud. No documented abuse incidents.

SmartSite.history: Smartsite (later Smartsite iXperion) is a Dutch CMS developed by Seneca, used primarily by Dutch government organizations and enterprises. It has limited modern activity.
SmartSite.summary_abuse: Smartsite has no notable abuse signal. It is an enterprise CMS used by Dutch institutional clients.

Smartstore.history: Smartstore AG was founded in 1999 in Dortmund, Germany. Its open-source e-commerce platform (Smartstore.NET / Smartstore) originated as a fork of nopCommerce and has evolved into a full ASP.NET Core-based e-commerce solution with CMS capabilities.
Smartstore.summary_abuse: Smartstore has no notable abuse signal. It is an open-source e-commerce platform for self-hosted stores, similar in risk profile to WooCommerce.

Smartstore biz.history: Smartstore biz is the hosted SaaS variant of the Smartstore e-commerce platform developed by Smartstore AG, a German company founded in 1999.
Smartstore biz.summary_abuse: Smartstore biz has a low but non-zero abuse risk as a hosted e-commerce platform. Like other store builders, it could be used to set up fake storefronts, but no documented abuse pattern is specific to this platform.

Smartstore Page Builder.history: Smartstore Page Builder is the CMS and page-building component of the Smartstore open-source e-commerce platform, developed by Smartstore AG in Germany.
Smartstore Page Builder.summary_abuse: Smartstore Page Builder has no notable abuse signal. It is a CMS component of a self-hosted e-commerce platform.

Smartsupp.history: Smartsupp was founded in 2013 in Brno, Czech Republic as a live chat and visitor recording platform for e-commerce websites, growing into one of the more popular European live chat solutions.
Smartsupp.summary_abuse: Smartsupp has no notable abuse signal. It is a live chat SaaS used by legitimate e-commerce businesses with no consumer-facing content hosting surface.

SmartWeb.history: SmartWeb was founded in 2005 by two Danish entrepreneurs, headquartered in Ega, Denmark, with the goal of creating Denmark's most innovative webshop platform. The platform grew to host over 6,000 online stores across Denmark and Norway. No notable ownership changes or acquisitions have been publicly reported.
SmartWeb.summary_abuse: SmartWeb is a Danish ecommerce SaaS serving small Scandinavian merchants. As with any storefront builder, there is a theoretical risk of fake-shop creation, but the platform operates in the EU/EEA regulatory environment with GDPR obligations. No phishing campaigns or fraud incidents specifically involving SmartWeb infrastructure have been publicly reported.

Smash Balloon Instagram Feed.history: Smash Balloon Instagram Feed is a popular WordPress plugin by Smash Balloon for displaying Instagram posts on WordPress websites, with over 1 million active installations.
Smash Balloon Instagram Feed.summary_abuse: Smash Balloon Instagram Feed has no notable abuse signal. It is a WordPress plugin for displaying Instagram content from legitimate accounts.

Smile.history: Founded in 2012 in Canada as Sweet Tooth Rewards, a Shopify loyalty app. Rebranded to Smile.io in 2017 after expanding beyond points programs to encompass referrals and VIP tiers.
Smile.summary_abuse: Smile.io has no notable abuse signal. It is a loyalty and rewards platform used by legitimate e-commerce merchants to drive repeat purchases.

Smile App.history: Smile App is the Shopify App Store listing for Smile.io, originally founded in 2012 as Sweet Tooth Rewards and rebranded to Smile.io in 2017. It is one of the most widely used loyalty apps on Shopify.
Smile App.summary_abuse: Smile App has no notable abuse signal. It is a loyalty program app used by legitimate Shopify merchants.

Smile Virtual.history: Smile Virtual is a telehealth platform focused on remote dental consultations, allowing patients to explore cosmetic dentistry options from home. It operates in the dental SaaS niche and appears to have launched around 2019.
Smile Virtual.summary_abuse: Smile Virtual has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche telehealth scheduling tool for dental practices, with no consumer-facing content-hosting or anonymous-access features that would attract fraudsters.

Smilee.history: Smilee is an Italian professionally managed live chat SaaS for customer service teams, launched around 2016. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes are documented.
Smilee.summary_abuse: Smilee has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B live chat platform used by customer service agents, with no public content-hosting features or anonymous access paths that scammers typically exploit.

Smilii.history: Smilii is a family-owned web hosting and domain registrar operating as a small independent provider. No notable ownership changes or industry events are documented.
Smilii.summary_abuse: Smilii is a small web hosting provider and domain registrar. Hosting providers carry inherent abuse risk as customers can use shared infrastructure for phishing pages or spam, though Smilii's small scale limits its overall abuse footprint. No specific documented abuse incidents are known.

Smootify.history: Smootify is a niche ecommerce tool that adds shopping functionality to Webflow sites using AI-assisted design. It was created around 2022 as a Webflow-native commerce layer.
Smootify.summary_abuse: Smootify has no notable abuse signal. It is a Webflow-dependent ecommerce add-on requiring the user to already have a Webflow site, which limits the attack surface significantly.

SmtpJS.history: SmtpJS is a free JavaScript library for sending email directly from client-side code via a relay server. It has been available since around 2015 and is maintained by a single developer as a side project.
SmtpJS.summary_abuse: SmtpJS carries an elevated abuse risk because it is designed to send email from JavaScript running in a browser, which inherently exposes the SMTP credentials to anyone viewing the page source. This design is commonly flagged in security research as a credential-leakage antipattern, and the library has been found embedded in phishing kits and spam campaigns where its relay server was exploited to send fraudulent email. The relay endpoint has no public rate-limiting or abuse-report documentation.

SmugMug.history: SmugMug was founded in 2002 by the MacAskill family in Los Gatos, California, as a paid photo hosting and sharing platform. In 2018 SmugMug acquired Flickr from Verizon/Oath, significantly expanding its user base and becoming one of the largest photography platforms online.
SmugMug.summary_abuse: SmugMug has a low abuse profile for a user-content hosting platform. It is a paid service with no free tier, which reduces the incentive for mass-phishing or scam-page hosting compared to free alternatives. Some image hotlinking misuse occurs but is not a primary concern. No notable public incidents of systematic abuse are documented.

Snabbt.history: Snabbt.js is a minimalist JavaScript animation library created by Daniel Lundin around 2014. The project has been largely inactive for several years and is effectively abandoned, with no releases or issue triage activity documented recently.
Snabbt.summary_abuse: Snabbt.js has no abuse signal. It is an abandoned JavaScript animation library with no consumer-facing surface or network calls that could be exploited by bad actors.

Snap.history: Snap is an open-source Haskell web framework created around 2010 for building web server applications. It has low activity in recent years and is largely dormant, with Yesod and Servant becoming more popular in the Haskell ecosystem.
Snap.summary_abuse: Snap (the Haskell web framework) has no notable abuse signal. It is a backend web framework with no consumer-facing hosted surface.

Snap Pixel.history: Snap Pixel is an advertising tracking pixel created by Snap Inc. (parent company of Snapchat, founded 2011 and public since 2017) to measure cross-device ad campaign performance. It launched as part of Snap's self-serve advertising platform, enabling advertisers to track conversions from Snapchat ads.
Snap Pixel.summary_abuse: Snap Pixel is an ad-tracking pixel operated by Snap Inc., a major public company. Legitimate use dominates; it is primarily abused in the same way as other ad pixels where it may be embedded on fraudulent sites to falsely attribute conversions. Snap Inc. operates a public bug bounty and maintains standard advertiser AUP enforcement.

Snap.svg.history: Snap.svg is a JavaScript SVG manipulation library released by Adobe in 2013, designed as a successor to Raphael.js for modern browsers. Adobe created it as an open-source project but has not actively maintained it in recent years, leaving it in a dormant state.
Snap.svg.summary_abuse: Snap.svg has no abuse signal. It is a client-side SVG library with no server-side component or network calls that could be exploited by bad actors.

SnapCall.history: SnapCall is a French video AI customer support platform founded around 2018. It focuses on real-time video interactions for support teams and has no notable acquisition or ownership changes documented.
SnapCall.summary_abuse: SnapCall has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B live video support tool used by customer service teams, with no public content-hosting or anonymous-access features that scammers typically target.

SnapEngage.history: SnapEngage was founded in 2008 and has grown into a multi-industry live chat SaaS platform serving healthcare, financial services, and other regulated verticals. It has remained an independent company and is known for HIPAA-compliant chat offerings.
SnapEngage.summary_abuse: SnapEngage has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B live chat platform targeting regulated industries; its customer base consists of businesses rather than anonymous end-users, limiting exposure to fraudulent or scam use cases.

SnapHop.history: SnapHop was founded in 2012 as a recruitment marketing platform. The company is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, with an APAC office in Sydney, Australia, serving the Asia Pacific region under the snaphop.com.au domain. No notable acquisitions or major ownership changes are documented.
SnapHop.summary_abuse: SnapHop is a B2B recruitment marketing SaaS with no notable abuse signal. The platform targets HR departments and employers for candidate attraction; it has no consumer-facing hosting or anonymous signup features that would attract phishing or spam abuse. No documented abuse incidents are associated with this platform.

SnapHost.history: SnapHost is an anti-spam CAPTCHA web form protection solution. No founding details are publicly documented.
SnapHost.summary_abuse: SnapHost is an anti-spam CAPTCHA form protection service with no notable abuse signal. Its purpose is to prevent abuse.

Snapps.history: Snapps is an AI-powered website builder and hosting platform that emerged around 2021-2022, targeting small businesses with drag-and-drop tools and AI content generation. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes documented.
Snapps.summary_abuse: Snapps is a page-builder and hosting platform with a low starting price and no documented free tier, which moderately reduces phishing-kit hosting risk compared to free-tier competitors. As a site hosting platform, it could potentially host malicious pages, but no specific documented abuse incidents are known. The vendor's AUP and takedown posture are not publicly prominent.

SnapSea.history: SnapSea is a platform that displays travel destinations using user-generated content. No founding details documented.
SnapSea.summary_abuse: SnapSea is a travel UGC platform with no notable abuse signal.

SnapWidget.history: SnapWidget was founded around 2013 as a widget service for embedding Instagram, Twitter, and 500px social media feeds on websites. It remains an independent SaaS with no notable acquisitions or rebrands.
SnapWidget.summary_abuse: SnapWidget has a low abuse profile. It embeds social media feeds on third-party websites and does not host user-generated content directly. The widget may load third-party social content which carries standard social media privacy trade-offs, but no documented abuse patterns target SnapWidget specifically.

SnatchBot.history: SnatchBot is a cloud-based chatbot building and deployment platform founded around 2016 in Singapore. It offers a free tier and supports deployment across messaging channels. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes are documented.
SnatchBot.summary_abuse: SnatchBot has a low abuse profile. It is a chatbot platform primarily used for legitimate customer engagement. While chatbot platforms in general can be used to build spam or social engineering bots, SnatchBot has no specific documented history of widespread abuse and its free tier is limited in scope.

sNews.history: sNews is a minimal single-file PHP and MySQL CMS that was popular in the mid-2000s for lightweight websites. The project has had minimal development activity for many years and is effectively abandoned.
sNews.summary_abuse: sNews has no notable abuse signal. It is a self-hosted, essentially abandoned PHP CMS with no hosted service surface and no documented abuse patterns.

SniffURL.history: SniffURL is a visitor analytics platform. No founding details are documented.
SniffURL.summary_abuse: SniffURL is a visitor analytics and URL tracking platform. Analytics tools that track user behavior can raise privacy concerns and may be deployed without user disclosure. No specific abuse incidents are documented.

snigel AdConsent.history: Snigel was founded in 2012 by former Google executives in Dublin, Ireland as an ad monetization platform for publishers. The company received significant investment from CBPE Capital and serves over 200 major publisher websites.
snigel AdConsent.summary_abuse: snigel AdConsent is a consent management platform (CMP) for GDPR and CCPA compliance. It is used by publishers to collect user consent for tracking. It facilitates the consent collection that enables ad targeting but does not itself engage in deceptive practices.

snigel AdEngine.history: Snigel was founded in 2012 by former Google executives in Dublin, Ireland as an ad monetization platform for publishers. The company received significant investment from CBPE Capital and serves over 200 major publisher websites.
snigel AdEngine.summary_abuse: snigel AdEngine is an ad header-bidding platform that loads advertising from multiple demand partners. By its nature it involves heavy third-party tracking as part of ad tech. The vendor publishes an AUP and works with major publisher clients.

Snipcart.history: Snipcart was founded in 2013 in Quebec City, Canada, as an HTML/JavaScript shopping cart that integrates into any website without a dedicated backend. In 2021 it was acquired by Duda, a website-building platform, which continues to operate it as a standalone product.
Snipcart.summary_abuse: Snipcart has a low abuse profile. It is a developer-focused ecommerce SDK requiring code integration, which raises the bar compared to no-code storefront builders. No documented patterns of systematic fraudulent storefront creation via Snipcart are known. PCI-DSS compliance is relevant as it handles payment data.

SniperCRM.history: SniperCRM was founded in 2019 as a Nigeria-based ecommerce CRM and operations automation platform. The product targets small and mid-sized ecommerce businesses primarily in Nigeria, with pricing in Nigerian naira and integration with local payment gateways such as Paystack and Flutterwave. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes are documented.
SniperCRM.summary_abuse: SniperCRM is a niche B2B ecommerce CRM focused on the Nigerian market with no notable abuse signal. The platform manages order and customer data for merchants and does not expose anonymous hosting, URL shortening, or mass-email features that typically attract fraudsters. No documented abuse incidents or security researcher reports are associated with this platform.

SniperFast.history: SniperFast is an instant search engine for ecommerce sites, developed by Prestalia GAN srl, a small software company headquartered in the Republic of San Marino. It is primarily distributed as a Shopify app. No notable ownership changes or deprecation events are documented.
SniperFast.summary_abuse: SniperFast is a niche ecommerce search widget with no documented abuse signal. It runs as an embedded search component on merchant sites and does not expose user-facing content hosting, URL shortening, or email distribution features that would attract fraudsters. No known incidents of abuse have been reported.

Sniply.history: Sniply was founded in 2014 in Vancouver, Canada, as a URL shortener that overlays a call-to-action bar on any shared link, turning every shared page into a marketing touchpoint. It has remained an independent company.
Sniply.summary_abuse: Sniply is a URL shortener with a call-to-action overlay feature, which carries meaningful abuse potential. URL shorteners are routinely used to obfuscate phishing and malware links, and the overlay feature can be used to inject fake brand messages or deceptive CTAs onto legitimate third-party pages. The free tier with no KYC allows anonymous link creation. Sniply does not have documented proactive abuse monitoring and takedown responsiveness is unclear.

SNO Flex.history: SNO Flex is a WordPress-based website theme and platform by SNO Sites, which specializes in providing hosted newspaper and publication websites for schools and educational institutions.
SNO Flex.summary_abuse: SNO Flex is a school newspaper website platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves educational institutions with curated content management and is not an open-access hosting service.

Snoobi.history: Snoobi is a Finnish web analytics and visitor tracking service. It has been operating since approximately 2009, catering primarily to European and Finnish businesses. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes are documented.
Snoobi.summary_abuse: Snoobi is a B2B web analytics tool that collects visitor data on behalf of website operators. Like all analytics tools, it poses standard privacy trade-offs for site visitors, but it has no documented patterns of abuse or use in fraud campaigns.

Snowplow Analytics.history: Snowplow Analytics was founded in 2012 in London by Alexander Dean and Yali Saar as an open-source event data pipeline for behavioral analytics. It raised Series B funding and has grown into a well-regarded data infrastructure platform used by enterprise data teams who require full ownership of their event data.
Snowplow Analytics.summary_abuse: Snowplow Analytics has no notable abuse signal. It is a self-hosted or cloud-managed behavioral data pipeline used by enterprise data teams. Its primary deployment model gives operators full control over data collection, and it has no consumer-facing hosted surface that could be weaponized.

SOBI 2.history: SOBI 2 (Sigsiu Online Business Index 2) is a legacy open-source business directory extension for Joomla, developed by Sigsiu.NET. Development was discontinued in 2011 when it was superseded by SobiPro.
SOBI 2.summary_abuse: SOBI 2 is a deprecated Joomla directory extension with no documented abuse signal. It has been superseded by SobiPro and is no longer actively deployed.

SobiPro.history: SobiPro is a Joomla extension for directory and listing management developed by Sigsiu.NET, a small German software shop. It has been available since approximately 2007 and continues to be maintained as a niche Joomla add-on.
SobiPro.summary_abuse: SobiPro has no notable abuse signal. It is a self-hosted Joomla extension for directory management with no hosted cloud component that scammers could exploit directly.

Sobot Live Chat.history: Sobot is a Chinese customer engagement and live chat SaaS platform, founded around 2015. It targets enterprise customers with omnichannel support features and has expanded internationally. No notable acquisitions are documented.
Sobot Live Chat.summary_abuse: Sobot Live Chat has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B live chat and customer service platform used by businesses for legitimate customer engagement, with no anonymous-access or public content-hosting features.

Socedo.history: Socedo was a B2B demand generation platform founded around 2013 that automated social media lead generation for sales teams. The brand name now redirects to Socedo Agency, suggesting a possible pivot or rebranding.
Socedo.summary_abuse: Socedo automates social media interactions to identify and engage leads, a capability that carries some potential for social-media spam or unsolicited outreach at scale. However, it targets B2B use cases and no documented incidents of systematic platform abuse are known.

SOCi.history: SOCi was founded in 2012 as a multi-location marketing platform. It has raised significant venture capital funding and expanded its platform to include AI-driven marketing automation for franchise and multi-location brands.
SOCi.summary_abuse: SOCi is a multi-location marketing automation platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B tool for enterprises managing local marketing at scale with no consumer-facing hosting surface.

Social Pinpoint.history: Social Pinpoint was founded in 2013 in Newcastle, Australia, as a digital community engagement platform for government bodies and consultancies. In March 2026, Social Pinpoint merged with Consultation Manager to form the unified Open Point platform, with Social Pinpoint rebranded as Social Point under the new parent entity Open Point.
Social Pinpoint.summary_abuse: Social Pinpoint is a community engagement and public consultation SaaS used by government agencies and large organizations. Its audience is professional stakeholders and public participants in formal engagement processes, not anonymous users, which significantly limits abuse potential. No fraud or phishing incidents involving the Social Pinpoint platform have been publicly documented.

Social Snowball.history: Social Snowball was founded in 2020 by Noah Tucker in Miami, USA, inspired by Tucker's experience scaling his own ecommerce brands through affiliate marketing. The platform launched on the Shopify App Store after 15 months of development. In June 2025, UK-listed digital marketing company Dotdigital Group acquired Social Snowball for USD 35 million, adding it to Dotdigital's marketing automation portfolio.
Social Snowball.summary_abuse: Social Snowball is an affiliate and referral marketing SaaS for ecommerce brands. While affiliate marketing systems can occasionally be exploited for commission fraud or fake referrals, Social Snowball's client base consists of established DTC brands on Shopify. No documented fraud incidents or platform abuse specifically tied to Social Snowball have been publicly reported.

Social9.history: Social9 is an Indian social sharing widgets and plugins provider, offering sharing buttons and content widgets for websites. It has operated since approximately 2015 as an independent company.
Social9.summary_abuse: Social9 has a low abuse profile. It provides social sharing widgets embedded on websites. The widgets may load third-party sharing code with standard tracking trade-offs, but no specific documented abuse patterns targeting Social9 are known.

SocialJuice.history: SocialJuice is a testimonial collection SaaS launched around 2021, enabling businesses to collect and display video and text testimonials from clients. It is an independent bootstrapped product.
SocialJuice.summary_abuse: SocialJuice has no notable abuse signal. It is a testimonial collection tool for businesses. Review and testimonial platforms can be used for fake reviews, but SocialJuice targets B2B clients collecting their own customer testimonials, limiting the attack surface.

SocialLadder.history: Founded in 2014 and headquartered in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, SocialLadder is a brand ambassador management platform that raised $4.55M in total funding including a $3M investment in August 2023.
SocialLadder.summary_abuse: SocialLadder is a B2B marketing platform for managing brand ambassadors and influencers. It has no notable abuse signal; it serves brands directly and does not provide consumer-facing hosting or free-tier accounts that could be exploited for phishing or scam campaigns.

Sociavore.history: Sociavore is a restaurant website and online ordering platform founded around 2019; it targets independent restaurants and food halls with commission-free ordering tools and no notable ownership changes.
Sociavore.summary_abuse: Sociavore is a niche B2B SaaS for restaurants and has no notable abuse signal. It does not offer free-tier hosting or consumer-facing URL shortening, and its customer base consists of verified restaurant businesses.

Societe des Avis Garantis.history: Founded in 2017 as a French startup specializing in verified customer reviews for e-commerce merchants, with its commercial and technical team based in France; no notable ownership changes since founding.
Societe des Avis Garantis.summary_abuse: Societe des Avis Garantis is a B2B review platform for e-commerce merchants. Some users have raised concerns about moderation practices and whether negative reviews are properly surfaced, but there is no documented abuse for phishing or fraud campaigns. The platform targets verified paying customers only.

Socioh.history: Founded in 2012 by Karan Jassar in Portland, Oregon, initially to help eBay and Etsy sellers advertise online; the platform evolved into Socioh, a Meta catalog ad optimization tool for ecommerce brands, with early seed funding including $1M from WhatsApp co-founder Brian Acton in 2016.
Socioh.summary_abuse: Socioh is a retargeting and catalog ad platform for ecommerce merchants and has no notable abuse signal. As a B2B tool requiring merchant accounts and ad network credentials, it does not provide free hosting or consumer-facing surfaces that scammers typically exploit.

Socital.history: Founded in 2016 and headquartered in Sutton, United Kingdom, Socital is an onsite campaign and popup builder for ecommerce stores, focused on lead capture and cart abandonment recovery; no notable ownership changes.
Socital.summary_abuse: Socital is a popup and lead-capture tool for ecommerce operators and has no notable abuse signal. It does not provide free hosting or consumer-facing content hosting that would make it attractive for phishing or fraud campaigns.

Socket.io.history: Socket.IO was created in 2010 by Guillermo Rauch under the LearnBoost startup to bring real-time bidirectional event-based communication to Node.js applications. It became one of the most widely used real-time communication libraries on the web and is now maintained by a small open-source team.
Socket.io.summary_abuse: Socket.IO is a foundational real-time communication library with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal. It is a backend library used by developers to build applications.

SockJS.history: SockJS was created around 2011 as a WebSocket emulation library for browsers that could not support native WebSockets, providing a WebSocket-like API with transparent fallback to other transports. It has no commercial backing and is maintained as a community OSS project.
SockJS.summary_abuse: SockJS is a low-level browser communication library with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

Socraft-UI.history: Socraft-UI is a small open-source UI component library hosted on GitHub by the socraft-public organization; no notable history or commercial backing is documented.
Socraft-UI.summary_abuse: Socraft-UI is a small open-source UI library with no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signal.

Socure.history: Founded in 2012 by Sunil Madhu and Johnny Ayers in New York City, Socure raised $650M in total funding including a $450M Series E in November 2021 at a $4.5B valuation, becoming a unicorn in the identity verification space serving financial institutions and government agencies.
Socure.summary_abuse: Socure is an identity verification and fraud prevention platform used by financial institutions and government agencies. It is not a target for abuse; its purpose is to detect and prevent fraud rather than enable it, and it has no free-tier or consumer-facing surface.

Soffront CRM.history: Soffront Software was founded in October 1992 by Manu Das in Fremont, California, making it one of the earliest CRM software companies. It launched one of the first commercial customer service software products in February 1993. Soffront has remained independently operated, expanding into white-label CRM, franchise CRM, and marketing automation over the decades. The company has offices in California and Kolkata, India.
Soffront CRM.summary_abuse: Soffront CRM is a B2B white-label CRM and marketing automation platform serving small businesses and franchise operators. Its use case is business-to-business sales and customer management, with no consumer-facing hosting surface that would attract fraud. No phishing, fake-storefront, or spam incidents tied to Soffront have been publicly documented.

Softr.history: Softr was founded around 2019 as a no-code platform enabling users to build web apps and portals powered by Airtable or Google Sheets data; it raised venture capital and became popular in the no-code community.
Softr.summary_abuse: Softr is a no-code website and app builder. As with most page builders offering free-tier site hosting, there is some risk of misuse for creating fake portals or phishing pages, but no documented incidents specific to Softr are known. The platform requires Airtable or Google Sheets as a data backend, which limits anonymous misuse.

SoftTr.history: SoftTr is a Turkish e-commerce software company based in Istanbul providing hosted storefront and domain registration services; no notable ownership changes are documented.
SoftTr.summary_abuse: SoftTr is a small e-commerce platform based in Turkey. It has no notable documented abuse signal, though it provides hosted storefronts which could in theory be used for illegitimate stores; no specific incidents are on record.

SoftwareSuggest.history: SoftwareSuggest is an Indian software discovery and review platform founded around 2014; it operates similarly to G2 or Capterra, helping businesses find software; no notable ownership changes.
SoftwareSuggest.summary_abuse: SoftwareSuggest is a software review and discovery directory with no notable abuse signal. It does not host user content or provide services that scammers commonly exploit.

Sogecommerce.history: Sogecommerce is the online payment processing platform of Societe Generale, one of France's largest banks. It provides secure digital payment gateway services for French and European merchants, backed by the bank's infrastructure.
Sogecommerce.summary_abuse: Sogecommerce is a bank-operated payment gateway from Societe Generale and has no notable abuse signal. As a regulated financial services product from a major European bank, it operates under strict payment card and financial regulation.

Soisy.history: Soisy is an Italian buy now, pay later (BNPL) fintech startup offering installment payment solutions for Italian e-commerce merchants; no notable ownership changes are documented.
Soisy.summary_abuse: Soisy is an Italian BNPL payment provider. As a regulated fintech, it has no notable abuse signal as a platform tool, though BNPL services broadly carry some fraud risk at the consumer level through synthetic identity abuse.

Sokrati.history: Sokrati is an Indian digital marketing and performance advertising platform focused on ecommerce businesses; no notable acquisition or ownership changes are documented.
Sokrati.summary_abuse: Sokrati is a B2B digital marketing platform for ecommerce brands and has no notable abuse signal relevant to phishing or fraud.

Soldsie.history: Soldsie was a social commerce platform founded around 2012 that enabled customers to buy products by commenting on social media posts on Facebook and Instagram; the company appears to have shut down or pivoted and its website is defunct.
Soldsie.summary_abuse: Soldsie was a social commerce platform that appears to be defunct. It has no notable abuse signal documented during its operational period.

SolidJS.history: SolidJS was created by Ryan Carniato, who began building it privately in 2016 and open-sourced it in 2018. Version 1.0 was released in 2021. It is notable for its fine-grained reactivity approach, avoiding a virtual DOM for direct DOM updates, achieving top scores in JavaScript framework benchmarks.
SolidJS.summary_abuse: SolidJS is a client-side JavaScript UI library with no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal.

SolidPixels.history: SolidPixels is a Czech website-building platform for businesses and agencies; no notable ownership changes are documented.
SolidPixels.summary_abuse: SolidPixels is a niche website builder platform. As with most page builders, it carries some theoretical risk of use for fake pages, but has no documented abuse incidents.

SolidStart.history: SolidStart is the official meta-framework and app router for SolidJS, developed by Ryan Carniato and the SolidJS team, first gaining traction around 2022 as SolidJS's answer to Next.js and Remix.
SolidStart.summary_abuse: SolidStart is a meta-framework for SolidJS with no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal.

Solidus.history: Solidus was forked from Spree Commerce in 2015 by Bonobos, Stembolt, and other contributors after Spree changed its licensing and direction. It became a community-led open-source Ruby on Rails ecommerce platform focused on high-volume, complex storefronts.
Solidus.summary_abuse: Solidus is a self-hosted open-source ecommerce platform with no consumer-facing surface operated by the project itself and no notable abuse signal.

Solitics.history: Solitics is an Israeli customer engagement and real-time marketing platform; no notable ownership changes are documented.
Solitics.summary_abuse: Solitics is a B2B customer engagement platform and has no notable abuse signal.

Solo.history: Solo (soloist.ai) is an AI-powered website builder that generates professional websites for small businesses with minimal inputs; a post-2022 AI-first product with limited documented history.
Solo.summary_abuse: Solo is an AI website builder offering free-tier website creation. Free-tier site builders with hosted pages carry some risk of misuse for phishing landing pages, though no specific incidents involving Solo are documented.

Solodev.history: Solodev is a US-based cloud CMS and web experience platform provider; no notable ownership changes are documented.
Solodev.summary_abuse: Solodev is a B2B cloud CMS platform with no notable abuse signal.

Solr.history: Apache Solr was originally created at CNET Networks in 2004 and donated to the Apache Software Foundation in 2006, where it graduated as a top-level project in 2010. It became one of the two dominant open-source search engines alongside Elasticsearch, widely used in enterprise search applications.
Solr.summary_abuse: Apache Solr is a backend search engine with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal. It is a foundational enterprise search technology used in data pipelines.

Solusquare OmniCommerce Cloud.history: Solusquare OmniCommerce Cloud is a French ecommerce platform built on Adobe ColdFusion; no notable ownership history or major events are documented.
Solusquare OmniCommerce Cloud.summary_abuse: Solusquare OmniCommerce Cloud is a niche ecommerce platform with no documented abuse signal.

SolutionReach.history: SolutionReach is a US healthcare patient engagement platform providing appointment reminders, messaging, and satisfaction surveys for medical providers; no notable ownership changes are documented.
SolutionReach.summary_abuse: SolutionReach is a healthcare patient engagement platform used by medical practices. It has no notable abuse signal; it serves verified healthcare providers under HIPAA constraints.

Solve Media.history: Solve Media was a CAPTCHA and advertising platform that replaced traditional CAPTCHAs with branded ads; the service appears to have been discontinued and its website is inactive.
Solve Media.summary_abuse: Solve Media was an advertising-CAPTCHA hybrid platform. As with CAPTCHA solving services generally, some risk exists for use in automating web interactions, but no specific abuse incidents are documented and the service appears defunct.

Solvemate.history: Solvemate was founded in 2015 in Berlin, Germany, as an AI-powered customer service chatbot platform. The company raised venture funding and built a presence in the European market. In March 2022, Danish customer service platform Dixa acquired Solvemate to add AI automation capabilities to its conversational customer service toolkit. The product now operates as Solvemate by Dixa.
Solvemate.summary_abuse: Solvemate is a customer service chatbot SaaS used by brands to automate support interactions. Its deployment model is B2B, embedded in client websites and apps, with no direct consumer-facing sign-up surface. No fraud, phishing, or platform abuse incidents involving Solvemate have been publicly documented.

Solvvy.history: Solvvy is a live chat and AI chatbot platform founded around 2015. It was acquired by Zoom in 2022 as part of Zoom's expansion into customer experience and support automation.
Solvvy.summary_abuse: Solvvy is a customer service chatbot platform now owned by Zoom and has no notable abuse signal.

Sonar.history: Sonar (sendsonar.com) is a US-based loan origination and point-of-sale software platform; no notable ownership changes are documented.
Sonar.summary_abuse: Sonar is a B2B loan origination platform with no notable abuse signal.

SonarQube.history: SonarQube was created in 2007 by SonarSource, a Swiss company founded by Freddy Mallet and Simon Brandhof, as an open-source continuous code quality inspection platform. It became the de facto standard for static code analysis in enterprise DevOps pipelines.
SonarQube.summary_abuse: SonarQube is a code quality and security analysis tool with no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal.

SonarQubes.history: SonarQubes appears to be an alternate Wappalyzer entry for SonarQube, the code quality and security platform created in 2007 by SonarSource (Switzerland). No distinct history from SonarQube.
SonarQubes.summary_abuse: SonarQube (SonarQubes) is a code quality and security analysis tool with no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal.

Sonetel Chat.history: Sonetel is a Swedish cloud communication platform founded around 2009, offering business phone numbers, chat, and call services globally; no notable ownership changes.
Sonetel Chat.summary_abuse: Sonetel offers virtual phone numbers and live chat for businesses. Virtual phone number services carry some risk of misuse for scam call centers, though Sonetel targets legitimate SMBs and no specific incidents are documented.

Sonline.history: Sonline is a beauty salon management software product developed and hosted in Russia. Limited public information is available about its founding year or corporate history. The platform serves hair salons and beauty businesses within the Russian market.
Sonline.summary_abuse: Sonline is a niche beauty salon management SaaS serving Russian businesses. Its B2B vertical focus and small market footprint give it minimal consumer-facing abuse surface. No documented phishing, fraud, or spam incidents involving Sonline have been publicly reported.

Sonobi.history: Sonobi is a US-based ad technology company founded around 2012, providing header bidding and programmatic advertising tools for publishers; no notable ownership changes.
Sonobi.summary_abuse: Sonobi is an ad tech platform operating in programmatic advertising. Like most ad networks, it loads multiple third-party trackers and contributes to user tracking on publisher sites, but no specific malvertising incidents are documented.

Soocommerce.history: Soocommerce is an ecommerce software development platform. No founding details are documented.
Soocommerce.summary_abuse: Soocommerce is an ecommerce software platform with no notable abuse signal.

Sophi.history: Sophi is an AI-powered content optimization and automation platform developed by Mather Economics, a US analytics firm serving media publishers; no notable ownership changes.
Sophi.summary_abuse: Sophi is an AI content optimization platform for media publishers and has no notable abuse signal.

SOPlanning.history: SOPlanning is an open-source project planning and scheduling tool, likely developed in France. It provides resource planning and task management for teams and is available as a self-hosted PHP application.
SOPlanning.summary_abuse: SOPlanning is an open-source project planning tool with no notable abuse signal. It is a self-hosted internal team tool with no consumer-facing hosting surface.

Sorry.history: Sorry (sorryapp.com) is a UK-based status page and incident communication service founded around 2012; no notable ownership changes.
Sorry.summary_abuse: Sorry is a status page service for communicating service outages to users and has no notable abuse signal.

Sortable.history: Sortable is a Canadian ad tech company founded around 2010 that provides header bidding and ad monetization solutions to digital publishers.
Sortable.summary_abuse: Sortable is an ad monetization and header bidding platform for publishers. Like all ad networks, it loads third-party trackers on embedded pages and is part of the programmatic ad ecosystem. No specific malvertising incidents are documented.

Sorted Return.history: Sorted is a UK-based SaaS company providing ecommerce checkout and returns management software for retailers; no notable ownership changes.
Sorted Return.summary_abuse: Sorted Return is a B2B ecommerce returns management platform with no notable abuse signal.

SOTA CRM.history: SOTA CRM is a customer relationship management SaaS with a freemium model. It is a small product with limited public history.
SOTA CRM.summary_abuse: SOTA CRM is a CRM platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a small business management tool with no documented history of exploitation.

Sotel.history: Sotel is an educational solutions company providing tools for e-learning. No founding details are documented.
Sotel.summary_abuse: Sotel is an educational platform with no notable abuse signal.

SoTellUs.history: SoTellUs is a US-based video review collection platform for small businesses, allowing customers to submit video testimonials. It has operated since approximately 2013 as an independent company.
SoTellUs.summary_abuse: SoTellUs has a low abuse profile. It collects video reviews for businesses and does not provide public hosting of arbitrary content. Review platforms can occasionally be used for fake-review spam, but SoTellUs targets small business owners rather than anonymous consumers.

SoteShop.history: SoteShop is a Polish e-shop management software platform offering both hosted and self-installed versions; no notable ownership changes are documented.
SoteShop.summary_abuse: SoteShop is a small Polish e-commerce platform with no notable abuse signal.

Sotoon.history: Sotoon is an Iranian CDN provider primarily serving MENA region customers; no notable ownership changes are documented.
Sotoon.summary_abuse: Sotoon is an Iranian CDN provider serving the MENA region. CDN providers can be used to mask the origin of malicious content delivery, though no specific abuse incidents involving Sotoon are documented.

SoundCloud.history: SoundCloud was founded in 2007 in Berlin by Alexander Ljung and Eric Wahlforss as an audio sharing and distribution platform. It grew to become one of the world's largest music streaming platforms, survived a near-shutdown in 2017, and received new investment from Raine Group and Temasek to stabilize.
SoundCloud.summary_abuse: SoundCloud is a major music hosting and streaming platform. It has had issues with spam accounts promoting fake plays and low-quality promotional content, and copyright infringement from unlicensed music uploads. The platform has content moderation in place but the scale means some abuse persists.

SoundManager.history: SoundManager2 is a JavaScript audio library created by Scott Schiller, first released around 2007 to provide cross-browser audio capabilities using Flash as a fallback. With the deprecation of Flash and HTML5 Audio API maturity, it has largely fallen out of active use.
SoundManager.summary_abuse: SoundManager is a browser audio JavaScript library with no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal.

Sourcepoint.history: Sourcepoint was founded in 2015 as a consent management and data privacy platform for digital publishers and advertisers. It raised significant venture capital and became a leading vendor in the consent management platform (CMP) space during the GDPR compliance wave.
Sourcepoint.summary_abuse: Sourcepoint is a consent management and ad-tech privacy platform. It injects consent banners and marketing consent flows on publisher websites. Research has documented that many CMPs including some using Sourcepoint deploy dark patterns in consent interfaces, though Sourcepoint maintains GDPR compliance tooling and provides publisher controls.

Sourcey.history: Sourcey is an open-source documentation static-site generator for OpenAPI, MCP, Doxygen, and Markdown, released under AGPL-3.0. It is a solo or small-team project on GitHub with no notable ownership changes or deprecation events.
Sourcey.summary_abuse: Sourcey is a backend documentation generation tool with no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signal.

Sovrn.history: Sovrn (formerly VigLink) was founded in 2012 and is based in Boulder, Colorado. It provides advertising and monetization services for publishers including data services, affiliate commerce tools, and programmatic advertising.
Sovrn.summary_abuse: Sovrn is a publisher monetization and ad network platform. As with all programmatic ad platforms, it embeds multiple tracking pixels and contributes to cross-site user tracking. Some programmatic advertising ecosystems have been used to serve malvertising, though no specific Sovrn incidents dominate the public record.

Sovrn//Commerce.history: Sovrn//Commerce (formerly VigLink) is the affiliate link monetization product of Sovrn Holdings, automatically converting regular links into affiliate links for publishers to earn commissions.
Sovrn//Commerce.summary_abuse: Sovrn//Commerce is an affiliate link injection platform for publishers. It injects tracking and redirects into links on publisher pages. Like all affiliate networks, it contributes to user tracking and occasional cookie stuffing concerns in the broader affiliate marketing ecosystem.

Spark CMS.history: Spark CMS is an Australian council/government website CMS developed by CouncilConnect; no notable ownership changes.
Spark CMS.summary_abuse: Spark CMS is a niche Australian government website CMS with no notable abuse signal.

SparkLoop.history: SparkLoop is a newsletter referral and growth platform founded around 2020, serving email newsletter operators who want to grow their subscriber lists through referral programs.
SparkLoop.summary_abuse: SparkLoop is a newsletter referral platform. Email marketing platforms carry some inherent risk of misuse for spam when referral incentives attract low-quality signups, but no specific documented abuse incidents are associated with SparkLoop.

SparkPost.history: SparkPost was founded in 2008 in Columbia, Maryland, as an email infrastructure provider spun out of Message Systems. In April 2021, Dutch communications company MessageBird acquired SparkPost for $600 million. After MessageBird rebranded to Bird in 2023, SparkPost became Bird Email as part of the unified omnichannel platform.
SparkPost.summary_abuse: SparkPost (now Bird Email) is an email infrastructure provider and is occasionally implicated in spam and phishing campaigns because attackers can abuse legitimate email delivery APIs to send bulk malicious email. SparkPost operates a free tier with volume limits, has published AUP and abuse-reporting mechanisms, and enforces sender reputation policies. Abuse is a small fraction of overall legitimate email volume, and the vendor has documented anti-spam compliance procedures.

SpatialChat.history: SpatialChat is a virtual event and spatial video meeting platform founded around 2020, gaining traction during the COVID-19 pandemic for remote events and team gatherings.
SpatialChat.summary_abuse: SpatialChat is a virtual event platform with no notable abuse signal.

Spatie Laravel Cookie Consent.history: Spatie Laravel Cookie Consent is an open-source Laravel package created by Spatie, a Belgian PHP developer agency, providing GDPR-compliant cookie consent banners for Laravel applications.
Spatie Laravel Cookie Consent.summary_abuse: Spatie Laravel Cookie Consent is an open-source Laravel library with no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal.

Spatie Media Library Pro.history: Spatie Media Library Pro is a commercial premium UI component extension for the open-source Spatie Media Library Laravel package, developed by Spatie, a Belgian PHP developer agency.
Spatie Media Library Pro.summary_abuse: Spatie Media Library Pro is a developer tool for file management in Laravel applications with no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal.

Spatie Support Bubble.history: Spatie Support Bubble is a small open-source Laravel package by Spatie, a Belgian PHP developer agency, that adds a non-intrusive support contact form widget to Laravel applications.
Spatie Support Bubble.summary_abuse: Spatie Support Bubble is a tiny open-source Laravel widget library with no abuse signal.

Spayee.history: Spayee is an Indian online course and digital product selling platform for creators; no notable ownership changes are documented.
Spayee.summary_abuse: Spayee is a course-building platform for creators and has no notable abuse signal.

SPDY.history: SPDY was an experimental networking protocol developed by Google in 2009 to address HTTP latency problems. It became the primary inspiration for the HTTP/2 standard, formalized in 2015 as RFC 7540. Google officially deprecated SPDY in 2016 after HTTP/2 was widely adopted, and Chrome removed SPDY support that same year.
SPDY.summary_abuse: SPDY is a deprecated transport protocol with no consumer-facing surface; it has no notable abuse signal.

SpeakPipe.history: SpeakPipe is a voice message widget service founded around 2013 allowing website visitors to leave voice messages; no notable ownership changes.
SpeakPipe.summary_abuse: SpeakPipe is a voice message widget with no notable abuse signal.

Speca.history: Speca launched around 2019 as a cloud-based API design and documentation platform supporting OpenAPI/Swagger formats; no notable ownership changes or major incidents have been recorded.
Speca.summary_abuse: Speca is a developer-focused API documentation tool with no notable abuse signal; it has no consumer-facing surface that would attract phishing kits or fraud campaigns.

Spectate.history: Spectate is a UK-based inbound marketing software platform that integrates multiple marketing channels; no notable acquisitions or ownership changes documented.
Spectate.summary_abuse: Spectate is a marketing automation SaaS with no notable abuse signal. Its primary use is inbound marketing for businesses and it has no consumer-facing content hosting features that would attract scammers.

Spectra.history: Spectra (formerly Ultimate Addons for Gutenberg) was developed by Brainstorm Force, an Indian WordPress company, and rebranded as Spectra in 2021 to better reflect its broader block-building capabilities for the Gutenberg editor.
Spectra.summary_abuse: Spectra is a WordPress plugin for building Gutenberg blocks with no notable abuse signal. As a self-hosted WordPress plugin, it has no consumer-facing surface that attracts scammers or fraudsters.

Spectro.history: Spectro was founded around 2000 as a French digital marketing agency called Ciblo that later pivoted to commercializing the Spectro ecommerce analytics platform, headquartered in Paris; no major ownership changes have been noted.
Spectro.summary_abuse: Spectro is a niche ecommerce analytics tool with no notable abuse signal; it processes retail analytics data with no consumer-facing surface relevant to phishing or fraud.

Speculative Loading.history: Speculative Loading is a WordPress plugin released circa 2023 by the WordPress Performance Team, implementing the browser Speculation Rules API to enable prefetching and prerendering of pages based on user interaction signals.
Speculative Loading.summary_abuse: Speculative Loading is a WordPress performance plugin with no abuse signal. It is a client-side browser optimization tool with no content hosting or user-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

Speed Kit.history: Speed Kit is a German ecommerce performance SaaS developed by Baqend GmbH, which spun it out as a dedicated product focused on caching and CDN-based speed optimization for online retailers.
Speed Kit.summary_abuse: Speed Kit is a backend performance optimization service with no notable abuse signal. It operates as a CDN/caching layer for ecommerce sites and has no consumer-facing content hosting features that fraudsters would exploit.

SpeedCurve.history: Founded in 2013 by Mark Zeman in Auckland, New Zealand. Acquired by Embrace Mobile, Inc. in November 2025 to expand Embrace's web observability platform. No notable forks, rebrands, or deprecation events.
SpeedCurve.summary_abuse: SpeedCurve is a front-end performance monitoring SaaS with no notable abuse signal. Its RUM script collects page-load performance data from real users and is not designed for content hosting, URL redirect, or mass communication. The service requires account signup and offers no anonymous or KYC-free access. As a monitoring tool, it has no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters typically target. No documented incidents of abuse have been reported.

SpeedEcom.history: Founded 2017 by Cédric Anger as a Shopify agency in France. The Speedfly premium Shopify theme launched the same year. In 2024, the Ecom Sphere community was launched on the Skool platform for French-speaking Shopify entrepreneurs. As of 2026, the agency has served over 800 entrepreneurs. No ownership changes, acquisitions, or notable controversies.
SpeedEcom.summary_abuse: SpeedEcom has no notable abuse signal. It is a French Shopify agency providing store creation, coaching, and a premium Shopify theme to e-commerce entrepreneurs. All services require direct contact with the founder and are used for legitimate e-commerce operations. The Speedfly theme runs within Shopify's hosted platform, which provides hosting and security infrastructure. There is no free tier with custom-domain support, no anonymous signup, no mass-email or file-hosting features. As a French business, it operates under EU regulations. No documented abuse incidents or security-research reports exist for this technology.

Speedimize.history: Speedimize is a Shopify website speed optimization service and monitoring tool. It provides performance analysis and optimization for Shopify stores.
Speedimize.summary_abuse: Speedimize is a Shopify performance optimization service. It requires a Shopify store and business registration. As a site speed tool, it has no consumer-facing features or hosting capabilities that would attract abuse. No notable abuse incidents documented.

SpeedOf.Me.history: Founded December 2011 as the first HTML5-based broadband speed test, created by SPEED OF ME, LLC based in Nevada, USA. The service launched to replace Flash-based speed tests that were being phased out by Apple and Microsoft. It has remained a standalone API service without notable ownership changes or acquisitions.
SpeedOf.Me.summary_abuse: SpeedOf.Me is a bandwidth measurement API with minimal abuse signal. As a technical measurement service used via JavaScript API or subscription, it has no free hosting tier, no user-generated content, no file uploads, and no URL-shortening or mass-messaging features that fraudsters typically exploit. API access requires registration and domain whitelisting. The service collects IP addresses and test results but does not expose user-generated content. No notable public incidents of abuse have been documented.

SpeedSize.history: Founded in 2019 in Israel by Sagi Keen (CEO) and Dr. Vlad Malanin (CTO). The company developed patented psychovisual AI compression technology for eCommerce media optimization. No notable ownership changes or deprecation events since founding.
SpeedSize.summary_abuse: SpeedSize has minimal abuse signal. It is an enterprise-focused AI media CDN with no free self-service tier, no consumer-facing user-generated content hosting, and no anonymous signup. Pricing starts at $99/month on AWS Marketplace with annual contracts, screening out casual abuse. Clients are established eCommerce brands. The service processes media through URL prefix or plugin integration without exposing end-user upload surfaces. No notable abuse incidents have been publicly documented.

SpeedyCache.history: SpeedyCache was first released in February 2023 by Softaculous (Electron Technologies FZC) as a freemium WordPress caching plugin. The core plugin is licensed under GNU GPLv3 since version 1.1.6 (April 2024). It grew rapidly to over 600,000 active installations. No notable ownership changes, acquisitions, forks, or deprecation events have occurred since release.
SpeedyCache.summary_abuse: SpeedyCache has no notable abuse signal. It is a server-side WordPress caching plugin with no consumer-facing surface, custom-domain hosting, URL-redirect, mass-email, or file-sharing features that fraudsters typically exploit. The free and pro versions run on the user's own WordPress infrastructure, offering no anonymous signup or KYC-free service surface. Security vulnerabilities are handled through a Patchstack disclosure program, with several security fixes released in the changelog. There are no documented patterns of this plugin being used for phishing, scams, or other abusive purposes.

Spektrix.history: Founded in 2007 by Michael Nabarro and Matt Scarisbrick, two software engineers with a shared passion for theatre who built a cloud-based ticketing platform. First client was the Royal Academy of Dramatic Art in 2008. Expanded to the US with a New York office in 2014. Received investment from Foresight Group in 2018 and Norland Capital in 2022. Became a certified B Corporation in 2021. Migrated infrastructure to Microsoft Azure in 2021. Co-founder Matt Scarisbrick stepped away in 2018; Michael Nabarro remains CEO.
Spektrix.summary_abuse: Spektrix has a minimal abuse profile. As a B2B SaaS CRM serving arts and cultural organisations directly, it has no consumer-facing free tier, no anonymous signup, and no custom-domain hosting that fraudsters typically exploit. It processes payment card data (PCI DSS certified) through Adyen with built-in fraud protection. The company maintains an Acceptable Use Policy and ISO 27001 certification, and its direct contractual relationships with venues limit abuse surface. No notable public incidents of phishing, scam hosting, or malware distribution involving Spektrix have been documented.

Spexo Addons.history: Spexo Addons (formerly Sastra Essential Addons for Elementor) was first published on WordPress.org in May 2024 by Fast Themes, a division of SkyWebTech, an Indian web development agency with 15+ years of experience. The plugin was later rebranded to Spexo and has seen active development with regular updates adding new widgets, a popup builder, and AI-powered content tools. No ownership changes or controversies have been documented.
Spexo Addons.summary_abuse: Spexo Addons is a WordPress plugin that extends Elementor with additional widgets, templates, and extensions. It has no notable abuse signal — it is a self-hosted plugin requiring WordPress and Elementor to function, with no consumer-facing hosting, URL-redirect, mass-email, or anonymous-signup features that fraudsters typically exploit. No documented abuse patterns or security incidents have been reported.

Sphinx.history: Sphinx was created in 2008 by Georg Brandl for the Python project's documentation. It became the de facto standard documentation generator for Python projects, adopted by the Python Software Foundation and thousands of open-source projects. It supports reStructuredText, output in HTML, LaTeX, ePub, and other formats. No notable ownership changes or deprecation events.
Sphinx.summary_abuse: Sphinx has no notable abuse signal. It is a static documentation generator tool run locally during build time. It has no consumer-facing surface, no hosting capabilities, no network services, and no user-generated content features.

SpiceThemes SpicePress.history: Developed by SpiceThemes, a WordPress theme company founded by Priyanshu Mittal and Ankit Agarwal in Kota, India. SpicePress is a business WordPress theme available in both a free lite version on WordPress.org and a paid Pro version with advanced features.
SpiceThemes SpicePress.summary_abuse: SpicePress is a WordPress theme with no notable abuse signal; as a website presentation template it has no consumer-facing service characteristics that fraudsters target. The free version is distributed via WordPress.org with standard review processes, and the theme itself does not expose hosting, file upload, or mass-email features.

Spicy Rocket.history: Founded around 2020 in Tegucigalpa, Honduras as a web development, IT consulting, and website-builder platform. No notable ownership changes, acquisitions, or public events documented.
Spicy Rocket.summary_abuse: Spicy Rocket is a small Honduran web development firm with a website builder platform. No documented abuse incidents, phishing campaigns, or security breaches are publicly associated with the platform. The company publishes a privacy policy but no Acceptable Use Policy or abuse reporting channel. Registration appears to be contact-based rather than self-service, which limits anonymous signup abuse. The platform's small scale and lack of public visibility have likely kept it below the radar of bad actors. No notable security-research reports or scam incidents exist in the public record.

Spiffy Stores.history: Spiffy Stores was founded by Brian Kenn and Shawn Finlay, who formed Spiffy Internet as a design studio in 2001 and began building their ecommerce software in 2004. They launched a beta program in 2007 and the full Spiffy Stores platform in early 2010. The company is Australian-owned and operated, serving small businesses in Australia and New Zealand.
Spiffy Stores.summary_abuse: Spiffy Stores is a small Australian ecommerce platform serving legitimate small businesses in Australia and New Zealand. Its regional focus and small customer base limit exposure to large-scale abuse. As an ecommerce storefront builder it has theoretical fake-shop risk, but no documented fraud incidents or phishing abuse specifically linked to Spiffy Stores have been publicly reported.

Spikmi.history: Founded around 2018 as a Russian live chat widget connecting websites with multiple messaging platforms. The project migrated from spikmi.com to spikmi.org. Copyright notices end at 2022, suggesting the project may no longer be actively developed.
Spikmi.summary_abuse: Spikmi is a live chat widget connecting website visitors with businesses via messenger apps. It offers no custom-domain hosting, file storage, or anonymous mass-communication features. No notable abuse incidents or security breaches have been publicly documented for this service.

Spin-a-Sale.history: Launched March 2017 as a Shopify app for gamified email capture via prize wheel. Developed by a small independent team based in Seattle, WA. No notable ownership changes or deprecation events.
Spin-a-Sale.summary_abuse: Spin-a-Sale is a Shopify marketing app that adds a prize-wheel gamification overlay for email capture; its abuse risk is low. The widget collects email addresses and generates discount coupons for ecommerce stores. It does not host user-generated content, offer anonymous signup, or provide file-sharing features. The app operates within Shopify's app ecosystem and must comply with Shopify's AUP. No documented incidents of the app being directly used for phishing, scams, or malware distribution.

Spin.js.history: Created in 2011 by Felix Gnass as a lightweight, dependency-free CSS-animated loading spinner. Later maintained by Theodore Brown. Licensed under MIT; no ownership changes or notable controversies.
Spin.js.summary_abuse: Spin.js is a front-end CSS/JavaScript library that renders animated loading spinners. It has no server-side component, no user accounts, no hosted service, and no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters could exploit. It has no documented abuse history.

spin.js.history: spin.js is a JavaScript library for creating CSS spinner loading indicators without images, created by Felix Gnass around 2011. Largely superseded by CSS-native spinners.
spin.js.summary_abuse: spin.js has no notable abuse signal; it is a small visual UI library.

Spine.js.history: Spine.js was created by Alex MacCaw around 2010 as a lightweight MVC library for JavaScript applications, written in CoffeeScript. MacCaw published the book "JavaScript Web Applications" (O'Reilly, 2011) which heavily featured Spine. The project gained moderate traction during the early-2010s CoffeeScript MVC era but was gradually superseded by React, Angular, and Vue. Maintenance shifted to Adam Biggs and community contributors, with the last commit occurring in October 2017. The project has no commercial backing and has never been acquired.
Spine.js.summary_abuse: Spine.js is a client-side JavaScript MVC library with no notable abuse signal. As a frontend framework loaded from a developer's own servers, it has no consumer-facing attack surface, no free tier, no content hosting, and no URL-redirect or mass-email capabilities that fraudsters could exploit. There are no documented reports of Spine.js being used in phishing, scams, or malware campaigns.

Spinnakr.history: Founded in 2011 in Washington D.C. by Michael Mayernick and Adam Bonnifield, who previously built Giv.to, a fundraising optimization tool for political campaigns. Spinnakr raised approximately $1 million in seed funding in June 2013 from Andreessen Horowitz, 500 Startups, Point Nine Capital, and Sand Hill Angels.
